Cognitive Science (13)

Evidence from cognitive psychology about learning and transfer

77879606101620222324 Repeated 5 variants
The capacity of human short-term memory during language processing is limited to approximately five unitary items.
The psychological claim regarding the strict capacity limits of human short-term/working memory (4–7 items) is consistently cited as a foundational constraint.
5 variants across 4 books
WKM Human working memory is limited.
PoC The capacity of human short-term memory during language processing is limited to approximately five unitary items.
AE Human short-term memory capacity is severely limited to approximately four to seven separate items.
CL Human short-term memory is limited to holding approximately five or six individual items.
PoC In the context of language, the item limit of short-term memory is approximately five items.
77879606101620222324 Refined 5 variants
The principle of the domain-specificity of skills states that expertise in one area does not transfer to another without specific domain knowledge.
The 1996 principle of non-transferable cognitive skills is refined into a more formal definition of 'domain-specificity' in the 2022 text.
5 variants across 3 books
SWN Cognitive skills are not transferred from one domain to another.
WKM Human skills are domain specific and do not transfer readily from one subject area to another.
AE Human skills and expertise are domain-specific and generally not transferable from one area to another.
AE The principle of the domain-specificity of skills states that expertise in one area does not transfer to another with...
AE Skills are domain-specific and expertise does not transfer between unrelated fields.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 3 variants
The human mind stores the full model of a text's constructed meaning in long-term memory rather than its literal wording.
The author maintains the consistent cognitive claim across 35 years that mental models of meaning are stored in long-term memory rather than literal wording (CL 1987, KD 2006, AE 2022).
3 variants across 3 books
KD The constructed situation model constitutes the actual understood meaning of a text.
AE The human mind stores the full model of a text's constructed meaning in long-term memory rather than its literal word...
CL People store the fuller model of a text's meaning in long-term memory rather than just the explicit words.
77879606101620222324 Broadened 3 variants
The necessity of shared knowledge is a direct prediction of the interaction between constrained short-term memory and capacious long-term memory.
Early technical observations on short-term memory and readability (1977) were broadened into a foundational cognitive justification for the necessity of shared knowledge (2023).
3 variants across 2 books
PoC There is a dependable correlation between absolute readability and absolute listenability.
PoC There is a direct connection between the limited capacity of short-term memory and the readability of linguistic clau...
SK The necessity of shared knowledge is a direct prediction of the interaction between constrained short-term memory and...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Reading proficiency depends on using schemas in long-term memory to overcome the limitations of working memory.
The 2020 claim that 'chunking' is the only way to manage working memory is refined in 2023 by identifying 'schemas in long-term memory' as the mechanism.
HtEC Chunking is the only method to overcome the severe limitations of working memory.
SK Reading proficiency depends on using schemas in long-term memory to overcome the limitations of working memory.
+ 8 more arguments in this theme
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
When required schemata are not quickly available, the cognitive load exceeds the limits of short-term memory, causing reading comprehension to break down.
The core claim that lack of schemata causes short-term memory overload and reading failure remains identical from 1987 (CL) to 2022 (AE).
AE When appropriate schemata are not quickly available, the limits of short-term memory are reached and the reading proc...
CL When required schemata are not quickly available, the cognitive load exceeds the limits of short-term memory, causing...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Readers operate within a narrow window of attention that is constrained by the limits of short-term memory.
The 2022 (AE) text adds the specific nuance that schemata act as a 'shorthand' for memory, refining the 1987 (CL) general observation about memory constraints.
AE Schemata serve as a mental shorthand that helps readers stay within the limits of short-term memory.
CL Readers operate within a narrow window of attention that is constrained by the limits of short-term memory.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Chunking allows the mind to treat multiple discrete items as a single grouping, thereby resisting the built-in limitations of short-term memory.
The description of 'chunking' in CL (1987) is elaborated in AE (2022) to explain how it treats discrete items as a single grouping.
CL The cognitive technique of 'chunking' allows the mind to circumvent the built-in limitations of short-term memory.
AE Chunking allows the mind to treat multiple discrete items as a single grouping, thereby resisting the built-in limita...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Language is transferred from short-term memory to long-term memory as a shorthand recoding of the 'gist' rather than a literal recollection of words.
The concept of language being stored as 'gist' rather than literal words is repeated with nearly identical phrasing in CL (1987) and AE (2022).
CL Language is transferred from short-term memory to long-term memory as a shorthand recoding of the 'gist' rather than ...
AE Language is transferred to long-term memory as a shorthand recoding of the 'gist' rather than a literal recollection ...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Information that is most frequently needed is stored at the 'surface' of a schema and is most directly available for retrieval.
The claim is refined by specifying that frequently needed information is specifically required for 'communication.'
CL Information that is most frequently needed is stored at the 'surface' of a schema and is most directly available for ...
AE In a schema, the knowledge most frequently required for communication is the most directly available at the surface.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Experts perceive information in 'chunks' of five or six pieces per schema, which alleviates the burden on short-term memory.
The 2022 text introduces the technical term 'positional schemata' to explain the mechanism behind expert chunking.
CL Experts perceive information in 'chunks' of five or six pieces per schema, which alleviates the burden on short-term ...
AE Positional schemata allow experts to 'chunk' information, reducing the burden on short-term memory.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
The set of 50,000 rapidly accessible schemata serves as a mental index for a much larger volume of knowledge stored in long-term memory.
The 2022 version explicitly links the 50,000 items to the concept of 'rapidly accessible schemata' acting as a mental index.
CL A basic vocabulary of 50,000 words or idioms serves as a functional index for a much larger volume of knowledge in lo...
AE The set of 50,000 rapidly accessible schemata serves as a mental index for a much larger volume of knowledge stored i...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Content learning has not occurred until the content is fixed in long-term memory through content rehearsal.
The author maintains the core cognitive principle that learning is synonymous with long-term memory changes across SWN (1996) and HtEC (2020).
SWN Content learning has not occurred until the content is fixed in long-term memory through content rehearsal.
HtEC Learning has not occurred if there has been no change in long-term memory.
Teaching & Pedagogy (14)

How to teach knowledge effectively

77879606101620222324 Repeated 5 variants
The defining characteristic of child-centered education is changing the classroom configuration from desks facing the teacher to large tables where children face one another.
Across four books (2020-2024), the author maintains that the physical layout of classroom furniture is the primary indicator of the shift to child-centered education.

SK The classroom configuration where children face one another at large tables lacks a sufficient scientific basis.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 4 variants
Transitioning from a child-centered to a knowledge-centered curriculum requires a significant change in teacher mindset.
The author consistently emphasizes from 2020 to 2024 that the transition to knowledge-centered schooling requires a difficult shift in teacher mindset.

HtEC Moving from a child-centered curriculum to a knowledge-centered one requires a significant shift in teacher mindset.
AE Moving from a child-centered to a knowledge-centered curriculum requires a difficult change in teacher mindset.
SK Transitioning from a child-centered to a knowledge-centered curriculum requires a significant change in teacher mindset.
RE Transitioning from child-centered to shared-knowledge instruction requires a significant change in teacher mindset.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 4 variants
The 180-minute blocks typical of centers-based instruction are developmentally inappropriate because young children can only maintain attention for five to seven minutes.
The author repeatedly cites a specific 'five to seven minute' attention span limit for young children to argue against the use of independent work centers.

77879606101620222324 Broadened 4 variants
The composition teacher is only accidentally a teacher of linguistic correctness; their primary role is teaching literacy and readability.
Specific observations on the role of the composition teacher (PoC 1977) are generalized into a broader philosophical claim about the nature of literacy and interpretation (RE 2024).
4 variants across 2 books
RE The craft of writing is only half the story of literacy.
PoC The composition teacher is only accidentally a teacher of linguistic correctness; their primary role is teaching lite...
PoC Teaching literacy is inherently a form of linguistic social engineering.

77879606101620222324 Evolved 3 variants
The modern method of 'discovery learning' is identical to the 'project method' advocated by Kilpatrick in the 1920s.
The author shifted from discussing the principles of writing revision (1977) to a historical critique of 'discovery learning' as a failed 1920s methodology (1996).
3 variants across 2 books
PoC Learning to write is fundamentally identical to learning the principles of revision.
SWN The modern method of 'discovery learning' is identical to the 'project method' advocated by Kilpatrick in the 1920s.
SWN Discovery learning is the least effective method of instruction in a teacher's repertory.

77879606101620222324 Refined 3 variants
The teaching of cultural literacy cannot be haphazard or left to spontaneous integration; it requires explicit agreement on the materials, facts, and texts to be taught.

77879606101620222324 Repeated 3 variants
To effectively overcome home-based disadvantages, schools must replace the romantic concept of 'natural development' with direct instruction of shared knowledge.

AE To achieve educational equity, schools must abandon the metaphor of natural development.

77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Classroom techniques like group work and independent seatwork are often used to hide the fact that most students are not receiving the teacher's attention during individualized instruction.

77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Teacher-led whole-class instruction is a more effective method for literacy than providing students with bins of free-choice booklets on diverse subjects.

77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
The purpose of classroom 'centers' is to differentiate instruction and meet students where they are individually.

SK The purpose of classroom 'centers' is to differentiate instruction and meet students where they are individually.

Shared knowledge enables a teacher to use that collective foundation to convey new knowledge effectively to all students in a class.
Both books emphasize that a shared knowledge foundation is the prerequisite for effective classroom instruction for all students.
SK Shared knowledge enables a teacher to use that collective foundation to convey new knowledge effectively to all stude...
RE A shared knowledge foundation in a classroom allows teachers to convey new knowledge more effectively to all students.
Other Arguments (409)
77879606101620222324 Evolved 6 variants
The Romantic premise of schooling is that nature and instinct are superior to the 'meddling intellect' in guiding a child's education.
The critique of child-centered education shifts from a historical tracing of reforms to a more philosophical attack on the 'Romantic myth' that nature should guide development.
6 variants across 5 books
WKM Ideas are reflected in architecture.
SWN The educational reforms proposed in the 1990s are a continuation of Romantic ideas from the 1920s, 30s, and 40s.
RE The Romantic premise of schooling is that nature and instinct are superior to the 'meddling intellect' in guiding a c...
SWN Child-centered education is a direct legacy of the 'Herbart Society' and the Romantic movement.
AE The idea that nature is in charge of human development and education is a false myth.
SK Romanticism prioritizes Nature and Instinct over the analytical 'meddling intellect' in the education of children.
77879606101620222324 Refined 7 variants
Knowledge-based schools utilize instructional time more efficiently than child-centered schools because every instructional minute is devoted to content delivery.
The efficiency of knowledge-based schools is increasingly framed through the lens of 'instructional minutes' and the elimination of 'critical thinking packets.'

RE Knowledge-based schools provide more tangible opportunities to develop citizenship thinking than child-centered schools.
77879606101620222324 Refined 6 variants
Bi-ethnicity, defined as the combination of home culture and the culture of the national grapholect, is an inherent and permanent condition of modernity rather than a temporary transitional phase for immigrants.
The necessity of a national grapholect is refined into the concept of 'bi-ethnicity,' where mastery of national culture actually supports the preservation of home ethnicities.
6 variants across 4 books

SK Bi-ethnicity, defined as the combination of home culture and the culture of the national grapholect, is an inherent a...
AE Ethnicity is a learned trait rather than an inborn one.
CL Literacy is fundamentally a function of a national culture rather than a local culture.
AE Literacy is fundamentally a function of a national culture rather than a local culture.
SK Mastery of a national grapholect encourages rather than suppresses the preservation of home ethnicities.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 6 variants
Child-centered classrooms suffer from 'pocketed' or 'isolated' learning where subjects are disconnected from one another.

AE Child-centered classrooms are characterized by pocketed and isolated learning where subjects remain disconnected.

RE Child-centered classrooms result in pocketed, isolated learning where subjects are disconnected.

A chief aim of early education in the United States is to impart the shared knowledge and values of American literate culture.

77879606101620222324 Repeated 4 variants
The pressure to show proficiency on end-of-grade tests forces teachers to focus narrowly on skills rather than broader citizenship or shared knowledge.

77879606101620222324 Repeated 6 variants

77879606101620222324 Refined 5 variants
A school-transmitted culture, as opposed to a folk-transmitted one, is the only thing that confers usability, dignity, and self-respect on individuals in an industrial society.
The author maintains the 1987 core thesis while adding 'self-respect' and the specific necessity for 'modern nations' to his later definitions of school-transmitted culture.

77879606101620222324 Repeated 4 variants
There is a fundamental difference between the Enlightenment-era attack on dogma and the Romantic-era attack on rote learning.

77879606101620222324 Repeated 4 variants
Standard literate languages are different in kind from oral dialects and should not be viewed as merely class dialects.

CL National languages and national borders are codependent artifices.

77879606101620222324 Refined 4 variants
A common base of shared knowledge is foundational for societal communication and the promotion of equal opportunity.

4 variants across 3 books
AE Shared knowledge leads to greater equality of competence and of income.
HtEC Shared knowledge is the necessary foundation for competence, equality of opportunity, and the American Dream.
RE A common base of shared knowledge is foundational for societal communication and the promotion of equal opportunity.
RE Rejecting the principle of shared knowledge is a sin against the principle of equal economic opportunity.
77879606101620222324 Refined 4 variants
The ability to make progress in reading and learning through shared knowledge is fundamental to education at every level, not just early elementary grades.
The author evolves from discussing early childhood oral language to a broader critique of individualism in education, emphasizing that all learning is essentially societal.
4 variants across 3 books
HtEC The dominant philosophy in contemporary elementary education is individualistic.
SK The ability to make progress in reading and learning through shared knowledge is fundamental to education at every le...
WKM In the earliest grades, learning by being read aloud to and through talking and listening is fundamental to language ...
HtEC Early education is essentially societal in nature, not individualistic.
77879606101620222324 Refined 4 variants
The fact that other nations with high television consumption have not seen a decline in reading proficiency suggests that American educational theories, rather than social forces like TV, are responsible for the US decline.
The author moves from debunking speed reading to a broader argument that pedagogical theories, not social forces like TV, are the primary drivers of international literacy trends.
4 variants across 3 books
PoC The high speeds claimed for speed reading are illusory.
KD The fact that other nations with high television consumption have not seen a decline in reading proficiency suggests ...
KD Television and social forces cannot fully explain the decline in American reading proficiency.
HtEC The same educational failures seen in the US have occurred in other nations that adopted child-centered romanticism.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 4 variants
The written norm of a national language never functions as a simple codification of the dialect characteristics of a single region.
The author uses almost identical phrasing across five decades to state that national written norms are not simply codified regional dialects.
4 variants across 3 books
CL The written norm of a national language is never a simple codification of the dialect characteristics of a single reg...
AE The written norm of a national language is never a simple collection of features from a single regional dialect.
AE The written norm of a national language never functions as a simple codification of the dialect characteristics of a ...
PoC A written norm is never a simple codification of the dialect characteristics of a single region.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 4 variants
Good schools have stronger positive effects on disadvantaged students than on advantaged ones, while poor schools have stronger negative effects on them.
The author consistently argues that school quality has a disproportionately high impact—both positive and negative—on disadvantaged students compared to their peers.
4 variants across 3 books
KD Unproductive schools have a disproportionately negative impact on disadvantaged students.
SWN Poor quality schools exert a more significantly negative impact on disadvantaged students than on advantaged ones.
SWN Good schools have stronger positive effects on disadvantaged students than on advantaged ones, while poor schools hav...
MoA Low-performing schools have a greater negative effect on disadvantaged students than on advantaged ones.
77879606101620222324 Refined 4 variants
Aside from physical malnourishment, the primary disadvantage for K-8 pupils in disadvantaged circumstances is a lack of language and knowledge.
The author sharpens the argument by explicitly contrasting a lack of shared knowledge against innate ability or purely economic factors as the 'chief disadvantage'.
4 variants across 3 books
SK Aside from physical malnourishment, the primary disadvantage for K-8 pupils in disadvantaged circumstances is a lack ...
RE The primary disadvantage of K-8 students from low-income backgrounds is a lack of shared language and knowledge rathe...

SK The chief disadvantage of disadvantaged K-8 pupils is a language and knowledge disadvantage, not just physical or eco...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 4 variants
The principle that children learn best when they help construct their own knowledge is the distinguishing idea of child-centered education.
The author consistently identifies 'constructivism'—the idea that children construct their own knowledge—as the defining (and ineffective) trait of child-centered education.
4 variants across 3 books
SK Child-centered education is based on the principle that different children have different natures.
AE The principle that children learn best when they help construct their own knowledge is the distinguishing idea of chi...

AE Constructivism, or the idea that children learn best by constructing their own knowledge through projects, is empiric...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 4 variants
Acquiring historical knowledge is a necessary prerequisite for students to develop empathy regarding contemporary social issues like immigration.
The author consistently argues that historical knowledge is a necessary prerequisite for developing empathy toward modern social and political issues.
4 variants across 3 books
HtEC Historical knowledge provides a necessary foundation for students to understand and feel empathy for contemporary soc...
HtEC Acquiring historical knowledge is a necessary prerequisite for students to develop empathy regarding contemporary soc...
RE Acquiring historical knowledge increases a student's capacity for empathy regarding contemporary social issues.
AE Historical knowledge provides a necessary foundation for students to understand and empathize with contemporary polit...
77879606101620222324 Refined 3 variants
The primary aim of education and civilization is to guide and shape human nature toward humane ends, rather than simply following nature's course.
The author moves from a broad philosophical contrast between nature and civilization (SWN 1996, WKM 2016) to a more specific sociopolitical claim about the communal purpose of education (HtEC 2020).
3 variants across 3 books
HtEC Human education is primarily for the benefit of the group rather than the individual.
SWN The primary aim of education and civilization is to guide and shape human nature toward humane ends, rather than simp...
WKM The classical aim of education was to correct nature through civilization, whereas the romantic aim is to correct civ...
77879606101620222324 Refined 3 variants
There is a direct connection between the decline in writing skills and a lack of basic civic and cultural knowledge among students.
Initially framing the knowledge deficit as a social justice and general education failure (KD 2006, MoA 2010), the author later links this deficit specifically to the decline of writing skills (RE 2024).
3 variants across 3 books
KD The knowledge deficit in American schools represents a failure of social justice.
MoA A lack of civic and general knowledge is the most significant deficit in the education of American students.
RE There is a direct connection between the decline in writing skills and a lack of basic civic and cultural knowledge a...
77879606101620222324 Refined 3 variants
The public elementary school is the primary remaining institution capable of ensuring all children learn the shared knowledge required for a nation to be operational.
The author transitions from viewing school as the only policy-controllable institution (CL 1987) to emphasizing its unique, indispensable role in maintaining a functional, unified nation (MoA 2010, WKM 2016).
3 variants across 3 books
MoA The elementary school is the primary institution that prepares children for effective participation in the common pub...
WKM The public elementary school is the primary remaining institution capable of ensuring all children learn the shared k...
CL School is the only institution for acculturating children that is susceptible to public policy control.
77879606101620222324 Refined 3 variants
The idea that students have a 'readiness to learn' is valid, but this readiness is determined by prior knowledge rather than biological maturation.
The author maintains that 'readiness' is based on prior knowledge rather than biology (WKM 2016, HtEC 2020) and adds the observation that this mastery increases student self-esteem (AE 2022).
3 variants across 3 books
AE Acquiring academic knowledge increases a child's self-esteem.
WKM Academic readiness is determined by a child's prior knowledge rather than their biological age or developmental stage.
HtEC The idea that students have a 'readiness to learn' is valid, but this readiness is determined by prior knowledge rath...
77879606101620222324 Evolved 3 variants
Universal educational achievement with equity is a process that cannot be accomplished overnight due to the inherent gradualness of knowledge and vocabulary acquisition.
The argument shifts from the gradual nature of learning (KD 2006) and opposition to differentiated curricula (WKM 2016) to a more ideological critique of the 'religion of Nature' (SK 2023).
3 variants across 3 books
SK The religion of Nature is a false religion that cannot be relied upon.
KD Universal educational achievement with equity is a process that cannot be accomplished overnight due to the inherent ...
WKM A differentiated curriculum is harmful to student achievement and educational equity.
77879606101620222324 Evolved 3 variants
A middle-of-the-road approach that includes both phonics and whole-language elements is the most effective teaching method for reading.
The author moves from technical writing pedagogy (PoC 1977) to advocating for balanced reading methods (SWN 1996) and finally to the broad claim that comprehensive education is the best test preparation (MoA 2010).
3 variants across 3 books
SWN A middle-of-the-road approach that includes both phonics and whole-language elements is the most effective teaching m...
PoC Wide reading is a slow and inefficient method for learning the craft of writing.
MoA Providing children with a comprehensive education is the most effective method of preparing for a reading test.
77879606101620222324 Broadened 3 variants
Words and idioms represent systems of association that belong to the language community as a whole, not just the individual.
The argument expands from language communities sharing idioms (CL 1987, MoA 2010) to framing these shared systems of association as the defining characteristics of an ethnicity (AE 2022).
3 variants across 3 books
MoA Comprehension is not just a matter of knowing individual words.
CL Words and idioms represent systems of association that belong to the language community as a whole, not just the indi...
AE Words, idioms, and grammatical systems represent shared systems of association common to an ethnicity.
77879606101620222324 Refined 3 variants
The tradition of commonality in elementary schools was a primary factor in the United States' historical national unity and high reading scores.
The author connects historical national unity and success (HtEC 2020, AE 2022) to a specific contemporary policy prescription for a shared national curriculum (SK 2023).

HtEC The tradition of commonality in elementary schools was a primary factor in the United States' historical national uni...

AE A 'WE,' patriotic, and communal style of education was a contributing factor in the historical rise of the United Sta...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 3 variants
Human beings cannot reliably remember more than four to seven discrete items that have been presented once without rehearsal.
The technical cognitive claim regarding the 'four to seven item' limit of short-term memory remains constant from PoC (1977) to AE (2022).
3 variants across 3 books
CL Human short-term memory cannot reliably hold more than about four to seven separate items.
PoC Human short-term memory cannot hold more than about seven random units.
AE Human beings cannot reliably remember more than four to seven discrete items that have been presented once without re...
77879606101620222324 Refined 3 variants
Genuine equality of educational opportunity is impossible without effective, early compensatory measures that address the initial differential in intellectual capital.
The author evolves from advocating for compensatory early measures (SWN 1996, KD 2006) to arguing that individualized curricula are actually counter-productive to equality (WKM 2016).
3 variants across 3 books
KD Effective schooling is in itself compensatory.
SWN Genuine equality of educational opportunity is impossible without effective, early compensatory measures that address...
WKM An effective individualized curriculum in the early grades is an impossible dream if equality of opportunity is the g...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 3 variants
It is unwise to change established spellings once they are recognized in the body of printed literature, regardless of their intrinsic drawbacks.
The author consistently argues against tampering with established linguistic conventions like spelling due to their stability in printed culture (CL 1987, MoA 2010, AE 2022).
3 variants across 3 books
MoA Standardized languages are highly resistant to change once established.
AE It is unwise to change established spellings once they are recognized in the body of printed literature, regardless o...
CL It is unwise to tamper with established, universally recognized spellings despite their intrinsic drawbacks.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 3 variants
Word learning is an extremely slow, cumulative process for all children that requires many exposures to a word to gain confidence in its meaning.
The description of vocabulary acquisition as a slow, cumulative process requiring multiple exposures is repeated across KD (2006), MoA (2010), and WKM (2016).
3 variants across 3 books
MoA Word learning is an extremely slow, cumulative process for all children that requires many exposures to a word to gai...
WKM Vocabulary acquisition is a slow, incremental process that requires multiple exposures to a word in various contexts.
KD Word learning is an inherently slow and gradual process requiring multiple exposures in multiple contexts.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 3 variants
In child-centered schools, the majority of instructional time is spent exclusively on reading, writing, and arithmetic ('the big three').
The critique that child-centered schools over-prioritize the 'big three' skills at the expense of content remains identical across HtEC (2020), AE (2022), and SK (2023).

77879606101620222324 Repeated 3 variants
English spelling, grammar, and punctuation have remained remarkably stable since the mid-eighteenth century.
The observation that English grammar and spelling have been stable since the mid-18th century is used as a consistent historical fact across four decades (PoC 1977, CL 1987, AE 2022).
3 variants across 3 books
PoC Standard English grammar and phonology have not changed significantly since the mid-eighteenth century.
CL English spelling, grammar, and punctuation have remained remarkably stable since the mid-18th century.
AE English spelling, grammar, and punctuation have remained remarkably stable since the mid-eighteenth century.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 3 variants
All forms of syntactic structure are typically lost from memory within a few seconds of processing.
The cognitive observation that the physical 'surface form' of a sentence is forgotten almost immediately after processing remains unchanged from 1977 to 2022.
3 variants across 3 books
PoC All forms of syntactic structure are typically lost from memory within a few seconds of processing.
CL Surface forms of sentences are lost to memory within a few seconds.
AE The surface forms of sentences are typically lost from memory within a few seconds.
77879606101620222324 Refined 3 variants
The scientific literacy of American fifteen-year-olds dropped from seventh to fifteenth out of seventeen countries between the late 1970s and 1988.
The author uses various data points from the 1970s and 80s regarding declines in civics (CL 1987) and science (SWN 1996, MoA 2010) to support a broader argument of educational decline.

In France, starting preschool at age two rather than age four results in a reading achievement benefit for disadvantaged students of more than one-half of a standard deviation by the end of fifth grade.
Specific data on French preschool enrollment and achievement (KD 2006, WKM 2016) is generalized into a broader argument about the achievement advantages of knowledge-centered curricula (HtEC 2020).

77879606101620222324 Refined 3 variants
One-quarter of low-income third-graders in the U.S. have attended at least three different schools since first grade.

The great weakness of Progressive Education is that it lacks a coherent theory of social welfare, tending instead toward anarchy or extreme individualism.

A communitarian, knowledge-based approach to schooling encourages national allegiance and the idea that citizens are shared participants in improving the country.
The defense of communitarian education is refined by explicitly linking it to a knowledge-based approach and shared civic participation in later texts (HtEC 2020, AE 2022, SK 2023).

The implementation of constructivist theory in the teaching of language and literature has had a negative effect on student learning.

AE Developmentalism has a demonstrably negative effect on social justice.
RE The combination of skill and knowledge produces a multiplicative effect on learning.

Teachers in knowledge-based schools are empowered to tap into specific prior classroom experiences to help students build knowledge forward.

The author consistently argues that disadvantaged students require systematic building of prior knowledge to succeed (AE 2022, SK 2023, RE 2024).

77879606101620222324 Refined 3 variants
By the 1940s, Romanticism had largely replaced Enlightenment ideas as the dominant philosophy of American schooling.
The metaphorical claim that America was 'bred in Romanticism' (SWN 1996) is given a specific historical timeline centered on the 1940s in later works (SK 2023, RE 2024).

SK In the United States, Romanticism replaced Enlightenment ideas about schooling after the 1940s.
SWN The United States was born in the Enlightenment but bred in Romanticism.
RE By the 1940s, Romanticism had largely replaced Enlightenment ideas as the dominant philosophy of American schooling.
77879606101620222324 Evolved 3 variants
Whole-class instruction where all students are deeply engaged provides more effective individual attention than individualized instruction.
Early preference for small-group work (SWN 1996) evolves into a strong defense of whole-class instruction as a more effective egalitarian tool (WKM 2016, RE 2024).

77879606101620222324 Evolved 7 variants

Early distinctions between the logic of writing and speech (PoC 1977) are replaced by the claim that there is no fundamental processing difference between them (KD 2006).

PoC The logic of writing is distinct from the logic of thought.

PoC A private written note is functionally equivalent to oral speech.
PoC Written discourse is inherently privative in character compared to oral speech.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 5 variants
Short-term memory functions as a mental vestibule that allows the mind to convert the temporal flow of language into nontemporal structures.
The cognitive description of short-term memory as a mechanism for converting temporal flow into stable structures remains unchanged from 1987 (CL) to 2022 (AE).

AE Short-term memory enables the conversion of temporal language flow into stable, nontemporal structures.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 5 variants
Human beings tend to interpret experience through middle categories—classifications that are neither too specific nor too general—because they are the most efficient for action.
The psychological claim that 'middle-level categories' are the most efficient for human perception and action is repeated across 35 years (CL 1987, AE 2022).

77879606101620222324 Refined 4 variants

The argument for a shared school culture (SWN 1996) is refined to focus specifically on the role of common language and knowledge in public-sphere communication (MoA 2010).

Current classroom physical arrangements are a direct product of the 'child-centered' educational philosophy and its reliance on reading levels.
The broad identification of progressivism as Romanticism (KD 2006) is refined to explain how child-centered philosophy dictates specific physical classroom arrangements (SK 2023).
4 variants across 2 books

SK The educational philosophy of child-centered schooling is effectively a form of self-centered schooling.

SK Current classroom furniture arrangements and practices are the result of the 'child-centered' educational idea.
77879606101620222324 Refined 4 variants
Child-centered advocates prevent resistance to their methods by presenting a false dichotomy where the only alternative to their approach is mindless rote learning and boredom.
The author shifts from defending against false dichotomies (HtEC 2020) to making direct claims about the scientific invalidity and social consequences of child-centeredness (SK 2023).

SK The child-centered approach to schooling has weakened American unity.

The duty of schools to compensate for home environments (SWN 1996) is refined into a specific mandate to provide interpretive knowledge for citizenship (RE 2024).

77879606101620222324 Evolved 4 variants
Diversity within a nation is an aesthetic category rather than an ethical one, unless it is grounded in the moral principle of equality.
A simple validation of diversity (CL 1987) evolves into a complex critique that distinguishes between aesthetic and ethical categories of diversity (HtEC 2020).

The core argument that knowledge drives reading development was refined in 2022 by explicitly contrasting it against specific cognitive factors like eye movement and memory.

A technical linguistic theory regarding effort in speech and language evolution (1977) was generalized into a broader pedagogical principle that all learning requires effort (1996).

Child-centered schools tend to be compartmentalized and disconnected, with subjects like math, science, and history failing to relate to one another.

The national literacy level of every modern nation is dependent upon citizens possessing in common a strong shared national ethnicity.
The 1987 focus on mastering 'national culture' for literacy evolved into a more provocative framing in 2022 that equates literacy directly with 'national ethnicity.'
3 variants across 2 books
CL Mastery of a national culture is essential to the mastery of a standard language in every modern nation.
AE The national literacy level of every modern nation is dependent upon citizens possessing in common a strong shared na...
AE In the post-Gutenberg era, 'literacy' is a synonym for 'national ethnicity.'
77879606101620222324 Repeated 3 variants
In modern society, an individual's employability, dignity, and identity depend primarily on their education rather than land or faith.
The author consistently argues from 1987 to 2022 that education is the primary determinant of individual dignity and identity in the modern world.
3 variants across 2 books
CL Education confers identity on the modern individual.
CL The employability, dignity, and self-respect of individuals in modern society depend primarily on their education.

The development of reliable methods for evaluating writing quality is the essential prerequisite for all future research in the field.
General calls for reliable research in composition (1977) were refined into a specific endorsement of epidemiological research as the gold standard for education (1996).
3 variants across 2 books
SWN Epidemiological research is the most reliable type of research in education.
PoC Reliable research is the most critical need for teachers of composition.

The association between Standard English and social class is contingent rather than inherent; command of the language is a prerequisite for wealth, not just a byproduct of it.
The 2006 definition of Standard English as a formal civic language was refined by 2010 to argue that its mastery is a causal prerequisite for social mobility.
3 variants across 2 books
MoA Competency in Standard English is a prerequisite for reaching the top tier of society.

KD Standard English is the formal language of civic and professional life, not just the language of print.

A student's ability to find the 'main idea' is dependent on their ability to understand the specific text content, not on a formal procedural ability.

Topic familiarity is the dominant theme in language comprehension for both native speakers and second-language learners.
The role of schemata and topic familiarity in language comprehension remains a central, unchanged pillar of the author's work from 1987 to 2023.
3 variants across 2 books
CL Schemata function powerfully in both language comprehension and memory.
SK Topic familiarity is a key element in verbal comprehension for both spoken and written language.
SK Topic familiarity is the dominant theme in language comprehension for both native speakers and second-language learners.

The meanings of words are essentially brute facts that must be learned by rote because there is no logical connection between a word and the thing it identifies.
Broad philosophical claims about word meanings (1996) were narrowed to focus on the Romantic origins of these pedagogical ideas and the non-rule-governed nature of vocabulary (2006).

KD The fundamental idea of Romanticism is that there is no boundary between the natural and the divine.
KD Word meanings are not rule-governed and must be learned individually.

The decline of American literacy and the fragmentation of the school curriculum were caused by the dominance of romantic formalism in educational theory.

Children's poor performance in communication is due to a lack of shared information and social insight rather than insufficient cognitive development.
The author adds nuance in AE (2022) by introducing 'social insight' and the mechanism of 'strained cognitive capacity' to the original 1987 claim regarding information lack.

The core thesis that national languages are artificial, political constructs rather than natural developments remains unchanged from 1987 to 2022.

AE Project-based, constructivist learning is the enemy of fairness.
SWN After age six, the sequence of school-based learning is determined by prior knowledge and practice rather than biolog...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
There is a lack of fit between the content that needs to be taught in schools and the general reading ability currently measured by tests.
The author transitions from a general definition of illiteracy in CL (1987) to a specific critique of the misalignment between school content and standardized reading tests in KD (2006).
CL Illiteracy is a subcategory of the general inability to communicate.
KD There is a lack of fit between the content that needs to be taught in schools and the general reading ability current...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
It is impossible to administer a fair reading test in the early grades that is not curriculum-based.
The general concern regarding fair testing in MoA (2010) is sharpened in WKM (2016) to identify listening ability as the specific mechanism for future reading success.
WKM Listening ability in early grades is the primary key to later reading ability.
MoA It is impossible to administer a fair reading test in the early grades that is not curriculum-based.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
The United States military is the nation's most meritocratic institution, and it requires mastery of Standard English as a prerequisite for advancement.
A general claim about national functional requirements in CL (1987) is applied specifically to the meritocratic structure and linguistic requirements of the military in MoA (2010).
CL National unity is a functional requirement for the United States.
MoA The United States military is the nation's most meritocratic institution, and it requires mastery of Standard English...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
The idea of commonality in early schooling is fundamental to the educational ideas of Jefferson, Washington, Rush, Knox, Smith, and Mann.
The historical precedents of commonality mentioned in MoA (2010) are distilled into a philosophical principle regarding specificity as the source of educational coherence in HtEC (2020).
HtEC Specificity is the parent of commonality and coherence in education.
MoA The idea of commonality in early schooling is fundamental to the educational ideas of Jefferson, Washington, Rush, Kn...
77879606101620222324 Narrowed 2 variants
Psychological research has proven that there is no such thing as a reliable general expertise of 'critical thinking' or 'scientific thinking.'
A broad dismissal of 'critical thinking' and 'scientific thinking' in HtEC (2020) is focused specifically on the disproof of general 'scientific thinking' expertise in AE (2022).
HtEC Psychological research has proven that there is no such thing as a reliable general expertise of 'critical thinking' ...
AE Psychological research has disproven the existence of a reliable general expertise of 'scientific thinking'.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
To be a disadvantaged child is to lack the modes of speech, vocabulary, and shared knowledge of the national print culture.
The metaphor of vocabulary as 'currency' in CL (1987) is refined in HtEC (2020) to define socioeconomic disadvantage as the lack of specific national print culture knowledge.
HtEC To be a disadvantaged child is to lack the modes of speech, vocabulary, and shared knowledge of the national print cu...
CL A universally shared national vocabulary is analogous to a universal currency.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
It is dangerous to overemphasize basic literacy or task-specific literacy at the expense of wider cultural literacy.
The warning against over-focusing on basic skills in CL (1987) evolves into a proactive solution in RE (2024), where education is framed as the direct antidote to cultural illiteracy.
RE Good education is the specific antidote to cultural illiteracy.
CL It is dangerous to overemphasize basic literacy or task-specific literacy at the expense of wider cultural literacy.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Preparation for a reading test should be done indirectly by becoming a good reader of a broad range of texts through the acquisition of broad general knowledge.
The advice to prepare for tests indirectly through broad reading in KD (2006) is refined into a more active call for systematic integration of knowledge into reading pedagogy in RE (2024).
KD Preparation for a reading test should be done indirectly by becoming a good reader of a broad range of texts through ...
RE General knowledge should be integrated more systematically into the teaching of reading.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
A shared standard language is necessary in the public sphere for democratic communication and decision making, regardless of what dialect is spoken in private.
The author maintains the core position across MoA (2010) and WKM (2016) that a formal, shared linguistic register is essential for public and democratic life.
MoA A shared standard language is necessary in the public sphere for democratic communication and decision making, regard...
WKM The formal register of language taught in schools is the essential instrument of communication in the public sphere.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Membership in the public sphere requires mastery of formal codes of speech and the tacit knowledge that makes that speech intelligible.
The general requirement of formal codes for public sphere membership in MoA (2010) is illustrated with the specific example of competitive debate in HtEC (2020).
HtEC Success in competitive debate requires mastery of the shared knowledge of the American public sphere.
MoA Membership in the public sphere requires mastery of formal codes of speech and the tacit knowledge that makes that sp...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
A shared curriculum allows teachers to view their role through the lens of citizenship preparation rather than merely focusing on end-of-grade test proficiency.
The argument that a shared curriculum shifts the teacher's focus from testing to citizenship remains virtually identical between AE (2022) and SK (2023).
SK A shared curriculum allows teachers to view students as future citizens rather than merely test-takers.
AE A shared curriculum allows teachers to view their role through the lens of citizenship preparation rather than merely...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Minimal levels of everyday information are insufficient for the functioning of a modern democracy.
The observation that minimal information is insufficient for democracy in CL (1987) is strengthened into the claim that common learnings are a necessary prerequisite for it in SWN (1996).
SWN Common learnings are a necessary prerequisite for a functioning democracy.
CL Minimal levels of everyday information are insufficient for the functioning of a modern democracy.
77879606101620222324 New Evidence 2 variants
The Duncker radiation experiment demonstrates that structural problem-solving methods do not naturally transfer from one domain to another.
The theoretical claim that problem-solving does not transfer across domains in SWN (1996) is supported by the specific inclusion of the Duncker radiation experiment in WKM (2016).
SWN Problem-solving abilities do not readily transfer from one domain to another.
WKM The Duncker radiation experiment demonstrates that structural problem-solving methods do not naturally transfer from ...
77879606101620222324 Evolved 2 variants
The excellence of American universities is driven by a tradition of free speech, dissent, and iconoclasm.
The author shifts from praising the iconoclastic nature of American universities in SWN (1996) to critiquing the monolithic and stifling nature of educational expertise in KD (2006).
KD American educational expertise is monolithic and characterized by the stifling of dissent.
SWN The excellence of American universities is driven by a tradition of free speech, dissent, and iconoclasm.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Current early-grade education in the United States is characterized by a 'generally romantic flavor' that prioritizes the nurturing of imagination.
The critique of Romantic individualism causing grade inflation in SWN (1996) is refined to describe the broader 'romantic flavor' of early-grade curriculum and imagination in KD (2006).
KD Current early-grade education in the United States is characterized by a 'generally romantic flavor' that prioritizes...
SWN The phenomenon of grade inflation in schools is a consequence of Romantic individualism.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Current standardized reading tests are unfair to students who have low general knowledge due to their home or school environments.
The author clarifies in WKM (2016) that the 'unfairness' noted in MoA (2010) stems specifically from tests measuring out-of-school knowledge rather than school-taught content.
MoA Current standardized reading tests are unfair to students who have low general knowledge due to their home or school ...
WKM Current standardized reading tests primarily reflect knowledge students acquired outside of school.
77879606101620222324 Broadened 2 variants
Shared knowledge of the geography of the Americas—including major rivers, mountain ranges, and nations—is a prerequisite for civic literacy.
The requirement of shared geographic knowledge for civic literacy in MoA (2010) is generalized to the necessity of knowledge equality for total income equality in AE (2022).
MoA Shared knowledge of the geography of the Americas—including major rivers, mountain ranges, and nations—is a prerequis...
AE Income equality is impossible without high literacy and the prerequisite equality of knowledge.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Cognitive science establishes that broad, commonly shared knowledge is essential for effective language use.
The author uses cognitive science to support the necessity of shared knowledge for language use in both WKM (2016) and RE (2024).
WKM Broad, commonly shared knowledge is essential for effective language use according to cognitive science.
RE Cognitive science establishes that broad, commonly shared knowledge is essential for effective language use.
77879606101620222324 Broadened 2 variants
A dimension of unstated knowledge is a universal characteristic of all human language.
The observation of unstated knowledge in language in MoA (2010) is broadened to a general claim about universal human tribalism in HtEC (2020).
HtEC Tribalism is a universal human characteristic.
MoA A dimension of unstated knowledge is a universal characteristic of all human language.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants

SK Proficiency in one athletic domain, such as basketball, does not automatically transfer to another, such as tennis, b...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
All cognitive skills depend on procedural and substantive schemata that are highly specific to the task at hand.

AE All cognitive skills depend on procedural and substantive schemata that are highly specific to the task at hand.
CL Initial schemata are highly sensitive to context.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants

CL Critical thinking skills courses are unable to generate the specific factual knowledge, such as geographical names, r...
RE Human memories are fundamentally connected to specific, factual knowledge.

Social competence, including literacy and speaking, is fundamentally dependent on shared knowledge.
The reliance on shared associations for communication in CL (1987) is broadened to encompass the entirety of social competence in HtEC (2020).
CL Successful communication is fundamentally dependent on shared associations.
HtEC Social competence, including literacy and speaking, is fundamentally dependent on shared knowledge.
77879606101620222324 Narrowed 2 variants

77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
A general ability to learn, predicated on broad general knowledge and vocabulary, is a more practical tool for the modern economy than direct vocational training.

SWN A general ability to learn, predicated on broad general knowledge and vocabulary, is a more practical tool for the mo...
CL The modern educational distaste for memorization is unrealistic and more ideological than practical.
77879606101620222324 Evolved 2 variants
Written comments on student papers are likely the most effective device for teaching composition.
The author shifts emphasis from written feedback as the best teaching tool in PoC (1977) to the power of stories for young children in KD (2006).
KD Stories are the most effective vehicles for teaching young children.
PoC Written comments on student papers are likely the most effective device for teaching composition.
77879606101620222324 Evolved 2 variants
The child-centered school model is significantly more prevalent in the United States than the shared-knowledge model.
The author moves from analyzing political polarization in KD (2006) to analyzing the dominance of specific educational models in HtEC (2020).
KD Polarization in the United States is caused more by a lack of shared knowledge than by ideological differences.
HtEC The child-centered school model is significantly more prevalent in the United States than the shared-knowledge model.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants

AE The accurate interpretation of spoken and written language depends on both speaker and listener sharing a significant...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants

77879606101620222324 Evolved 2 variants
'Authentic' types of reading tests are uncontrolled, unfair, and unable to replicate real-world reading performance.
The author shifts from attacking 'authentic' tests in SWN (1996) to defending standardized tests as accurate indexes of real-world ability in MoA (2010).

SWN 'Authentic' types of reading tests are uncontrolled, unfair, and unable to replicate real-world reading performance.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants

French reading scores fell significantly and unequally across socio-economic groups following the Loi Jospin reforms.

SK French reading scores fell significantly and unequally across socio-economic groups following the Loi Jospin reforms.

RE In child-centered schools, learning is compartmentalized and different from student to student.

77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
The adoption of child-centered educational theories is a primary cause of current American political hostility and slogan-dominated discourse.

RE The adoption of child-centered educational theories is a primary cause of current American political hostility and sl...
HtEC The loss of educational commonality is a primary driver of the current loss of American social cohesion.
77879606101620222324 Evolved 2 variants
Standard language is the most effective tool for communicating with an unknown or unseen audience in writing.
The 1977 emphasis on standard language for communication evolves into a 2006 critique of the lack of a defined curriculum in American classrooms.
PoC Standard language is the most effective tool for communicating with an unknown or unseen audience in writing.
KD The curriculum in most American classrooms is currently an 'unknown curriculum'.
77879606101620222324 New Evidence 2 variants
The way humans process written words and spoken words is essentially the same, utilizing a 'phonological loop'.

PoC Listenability and readability are essentially the same functional process.
KD The way humans process written words and spoken words is essentially the same, utilizing a 'phonological loop'.
77879606101620222324 Broadened 2 variants
The educational utility of identifying multiple intelligence profiles is limited because there is no consensus on whether instruction should play to a student's strengths or bolster their weaknesses.

PoC The utility of a specific maxim is relative to the individual student's specific deficiencies.

77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
The primary function of literacy is to master the standard written language to enable complex communication across time and space.
The 1987 function of literacy is refined in 2010 to emphasize that specific, concrete knowledge is the necessary vehicle for that standard communication.
CL The primary function of literacy is to master the standard written language to enable complex communication across ti...
MoA Specific, concrete knowledge is required to enable communication in a standard language across time and space.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Writers take for granted that their readers possess a specific body of background information, such as knowledge of the Civil War.

Reading involves a two-way traffic between ethnic schemata and the words on the page.
The general 1987 metaphor of 'two-way traffic' in reading is narrowed in 2022 to focus specifically on the role of ethnic schemata.
CL A 'two-way traffic' exists between a reader's schemata and the words on the page.
AE Reading involves a two-way traffic between ethnic schemata and the words on the page.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Reading is an active process of selecting and adjusting the most appropriate schemata rather than a passive reception of meaning.

AE Reading is an active process of selecting and adjusting appropriate schemata rather than a passive reception of meaning.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants

AE A skill is not a unified system of intellectual muscles that can be developed by calisthenics into a vigorous all-pur...

77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants

AE National systems of education and written languages have amended the natural law of constant linguistic change.
77879606101620222324 Broadened 2 variants
The true variety of human taste is significantly greater in reality than it appears to be in public discourse.
The 1987 work generalizes a 1977 claim about 'subjective judgment' into a broader statement about 'human taste' and its presence in 'public discourse.'
CL The true variety of human taste is significantly greater in reality than it appears to be in public discourse.
PoC Subjective variety in judgment is greater in reality than it appears on the surface.
77879606101620222324 Broadened 2 variants
The divergence in reading skills between socioeconomic groups after age six is primarily caused by a lack of elementary cultural knowledge in low-income pupils.
The specific 1987 focus on reading skills in six-year-olds is generalized in 2016 to explain the broader national income differential.

An academic curriculum is considered an intolerable heresy by the dominant 'anti-curriculum creed' in American education.

77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
American intellectual history shifted in the early 1800s from a standard of divine law to a standard of 'nature' as the primary guide for human development.
The broad 2006 historical shift from 'divine law' to 'nature' is applied specifically to the 'Romantic movement' in 2024.
KD American intellectual history shifted in the early 1800s from a standard of divine law to a standard of 'nature' as t...
RE The Romantic movement prioritized nature over history as the primary guide for education.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Written language possesses a far more extensive vocabulary than oral speech.
The author consistently asserts that written language utilizes a significantly larger vocabulary than oral speech.
KD Oral speech tends to use a smaller vocabulary than written speech.
PoC Written language possesses a far more extensive vocabulary than oral speech.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
A strong majority of the American public recognizes curricular commonality as a necessary precondition for educational excellence and fairness.

77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
American civil religion is uniquely durable because it was designed to accommodate the secularism and religious criticism of the Enlightenment.

MoA The American civil religion is inherently antagonistic to racism, sexism, and jingoism.
CL American civil religion is uniquely durable because it was designed to accommodate the secularism and religious criti...
77879606101620222324 Evolved 2 variants
Correctness is an internal feature of a particular dialect, not an arbitrary standard that exists outside of the dialect.
The focus shifts from a 1977 linguistic theory about dialect internal logic to a 2010 socio-political claim about standard language as democratic property.
MoA The standard American language is not a class dialect, but an artificial construct that is the property of all citizens.

77879606101620222324 Broadened 2 variants
All modern national cultures are multicultural, artificial constructs sustained by schools and mass communication.
The 2010 claim regarding 'national knowledge assumptions' is expanded in 2016 to encompass the entirety of 'modern national cultures.'
MoA All modern national knowledge assumptions are arbitrarily invented constructs.
WKM All modern national cultures are multicultural, artificial constructs sustained by schools and mass communication.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
The child-centered approach to reading has deeply harmed educational equality and injured the cause of social justice.

MoA The child-centered educational movement has weakened American competence, solidarity, and equality of opportunity.
SK The child-centered approach to reading has deeply harmed educational equality and injured the cause of social justice.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants

RE Modern French education colleges redefine teachers as 'resource persons' or 'guides' rather than instructors.
77879606101620222324 Evolved 2 variants
Linguistic and visual perception remains meaningless unless fragments are placed within larger, not presently visible structures based on past knowledge.
The author shifts from a 2016 focus on administrative governance to a 2022 cognitive focus on how mental structures provide meaning to sensory fragments.
WKM Governance structures are not educational structures.
AE Linguistic and visual perception remains meaningless unless fragments are placed within larger, not presently visible...
77879606101620222324 Broadened 2 variants
Educational romanticism is a theory that views good education as a natural development conducted in accord with an individual child’s nature and interests.
The 2016 claim that natural development increases social inequality is broadened in 2020 into a formal definition of 'educational romanticism' as a general theory.
HtEC Educational romanticism is a theory that views good education as a natural development conducted in accord with an in...
WKM Natural development as an educational principle intensifies social inequalities.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Progressivism became the dominant philosophy in US teacher-training institutions during the early and mid-twentieth century.
The 2010 claim that child-centered ideas dominated training by the 1920s-30s is refined in 2020 with a broader mid-twentieth-century timeframe and the specific label of 'Progressivism.'
HtEC Progressivism became the dominant philosophy in US teacher-training institutions during the early and mid-twentieth c...
MoA The child-centered idea became dominant in American teacher-training institutions by the 1920s and 1930s.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Low American reading scores are primarily the result of incorrect educational theories taught in teacher-training institutions.
The core argument that low performance results from incorrect theories taught in teacher-training schools is repeated across both books.
HtEC Low American reading scores are primarily the result of incorrect educational theories taught in teacher-training ins...
AE The poor performance of schools is the result of incorrect theories taught in education schools.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
A child must learn the shared, unspoken knowledge of her speech community to understand the vast realm of the unstated taken for granted in human speech.
The 2020 requirement to learn 'taken-for-granted' knowledge is refined in 2023 with specific linguistic detail regarding speech communities and the 'vast realm of the unstated.'
HtEC To become a full member of society, a child must learn the knowledge taken for granted by that society.
SK A child must learn the shared, unspoken knowledge of her speech community to understand the vast realm of the unstate...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
In child-centered classrooms, the selection of materials and textbooks is often left entirely to the individual teacher rather than being guided by a curriculum.
The observation that child-centered classrooms leave material selection to individual teachers is repeated with nearly identical wording.

SK In child-centered classrooms, the selection of materials and textbooks is typically left entirely to the individual t...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Isolated critical thinking exercises, such as logic puzzles and analogies, fail to make a meaningful difference in student learning.
The assertion that isolated logic puzzles and analogy exercises fail to improve student learning remains consistent between 2020 and 2022.
HtEC Critical thinking packets consisting of analogies and logic puzzles do not make a noticeable difference in student le...
AE Isolated critical thinking exercises, such as logic puzzles and analogies, fail to make a meaningful difference in st...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
The first cohort of students who experienced a knowledge-based curriculum throughout elementary school achieved exceptional college success, including numerous awards and scholarships.
The success of the first generation/cohort of students under a knowledge-based curriculum is cited with the same evidence in both works.
HtEC The first generation of students to complete a full K-8 knowledge-based elementary education achieved exceptional col...
AE The first cohort of students who experienced a knowledge-based curriculum throughout elementary school achieved excep...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
It is significantly harder for teachers to make academic progress with students who arrive with disparate levels of content knowledge.
The claim that academic progress is hindered by disparate content knowledge among students in a single classroom is repeated in 2023.
HtEC It is significantly harder to make academic progress when students in a single classroom lack common content knowledge.
SK It is significantly harder for teachers to make academic progress with students who arrive with disparate levels of c...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
The culture of a knowledge-based school extends to the families of the students, influencing their activities outside of school.
The observation that a knowledge-based school culture influences family behavior outside of school is repeated with minor wording changes.
HtEC A knowledge-based school culture influences the behavior and interests of students' families outside of school.
AE The culture of a knowledge-based school extends to the families of the students, influencing their activities outside...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
The child-centered school model inverts the educational focus from shared knowledge to the individual child's interests, talents, and needs.
The 2010 observation about individualizing education is refined in 2020 to argue this model leads to total isolation and compartmentalization.
HtEC The child-centered school model causes education to become isolated and compartmentalized.
MoA The child-centered school model inverts the educational focus from shared knowledge to the individual child's interes...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
American education was historically more successful when teachers viewed students as future citizens rather than mere test-takers.
The argument favoring the historical view of students as future citizens rather than test-takers is repeated across both texts.
HtEC American education was historically more successful when teachers viewed students as future citizens rather than mere...
AE Historically, education was more successful when students were viewed as future citizens rather than merely test-takers.
77879606101620222324 Evolved 2 variants
The child-centered education movement was supported by parents who held an optimistic view of human nature derived from Wordsworth and Emerson.
The 2010 analysis of parents' romanticized views of nature shifts in 2020 to a call for liberating parents and teachers from those very doctrines.
HtEC Teachers and parents require liberation from the doctrines of child-centered education.
MoA The child-centered education movement was supported by parents who held an optimistic view of human nature derived fr...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Nature is not a reliable guide for educational outcomes and frequently fails to deliver the expected results.
The argument that nature is not a reliable guide for human behavior or educational outcomes is maintained across a decade.
HtEC Nature is not a socially reliable guide for human behavior, as the romantics believed.
MoA Nature is not a reliable guide for educational outcomes and frequently fails to deliver the expected results.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
The human neocortex is largely a blank slate that requires instruction from previous generations to function.
The 2020 'blank slate' description of the neocortex is refined in 2022 to emphasize its functional dependence on instruction from previous generations.
HtEC The latest-evolved part of the human brain, the neocortex, is largely a blank slate.
AE The human neocortex is largely a blank slate that requires instruction from previous generations to function.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Individuals inhabit multiple local cultures and cultural roles simultaneously within the larger national culture.
The 1987 core idea that individuals inhabit multiple cultural identities simultaneously is maintained in the 2020 text.
HtEC Individuals can adopt and maintain multiple cultural identities simultaneously.
CL Individuals inhabit multiple local cultures and cultural roles simultaneously within the larger national culture.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Standard American English is a neutral tool for the public sphere and is not the property of any specific social group.
The 2010 claim that Standard English is a 'concrete reality' is refined in 2020 into a more political argument about its status as a 'neutral tool' for the public sphere.
HtEC Standard American English is a neutral tool for the public sphere and is not the property of any specific social group.
MoA Standard American English is a concrete and stable reality of American life, not a myth.
77879606101620222324 Broadened 2 variants
Technical excellence and fairness in education are dependent upon the high degree of coherence and specificity found in curricula like Singapore's.
The 2010 focus on fairness starting in the 'early grades' is broadened in 2020 to a claim about the systemic 'coherence and specificity' of international curricula like Singapore's.
HtEC Technical excellence and fairness in education are dependent upon the high degree of coherence and specificity found ...
MoA The foundations of fairness and high achievement in education are established specifically in the early grades.
77879606101620222324 Evolved 2 variants
Effective performance in public debate requires mastery of 'public language' and its publicly shared associations, which the author defines as shared ethnicity.
The author shifts from a 1987 focus on linguistic conservatism for radical communication to a 2022 argument about shared ethnicity and public language mastery.
AE Effective performance in public debate requires mastery of 'public language' and its publicly shared associations, wh...
CL Effective radical communication requires conservatism in the means of communication (language and spelling).
77879606101620222324 Evolved 2 variants
The rise of developmentalism as a guiding principle in early education is the greatest tragedy of American public schooling in the last century.
The 2016 celebration of the tradition of commonality shifts to a 2022 tragedy-focused critique of the rise of developmentalism.
AE The rise of developmentalism as a guiding principle in early education is the greatest tragedy of American public sch...
WKM The concept of commonality in early schooling is a deeply rooted American tradition.
77879606101620222324 Evolved 2 variants

AE Intellectual enfranchisement leads to financial enfranchisement.
SWN Adequate attention to the transmission of broad general knowledge paradoxically leads to the possession of general in...
77879606101620222324 Broadened 2 variants
The French educational transition in the 1980s is the most decisive educational experiment in history due to its massive scale and reliable record keeping.

AE The French educational transition in the 1980s is the most decisive educational experiment in history due to its mass...
RE The French and American educational shifts constitute a two-nation experiment that is the most decisive educational e...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Successful communication in any community depends on commonly shared prior knowledge.
The author maintains the same core argument that successful communication requires shared prior knowledge or associations across 2020 (HtEC) and 2022 (AE).
AE Successful communication depends upon shared associations.
HtEC Successful communication in any community depends on commonly shared prior knowledge.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Literate adults perform communication tasks involving unfamiliar shapes with virtually no errors on the first attempt.
The 2022 (AE) version removes the 1987 (CL) qualification 'on the first attempt,' presenting the observation of errorless communication regarding unfamiliar shapes as a more general fact.
AE Literate adults perform communication tasks involving unfamiliar shapes with virtually no errors.
CL Literate adults perform communication tasks involving unfamiliar shapes with virtually no errors on the first attempt.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
American schooling departed from its Enlightenment roots (John Locke) to adopt the Romantic approach to education in the 1940s.
The argument that the U.S. abandoned its Enlightenment/Lockean roots for Romanticism in the 1940s is consistently maintained between 2023 (SK) and 2024 (RE).
SK American schooling departed from its Enlightenment roots (John Locke) to adopt the Romantic approach to education in ...
RE During the 1940s, the United States fully parted from its intellectual roots in John Locke and the Enlightenment.
77879606101620222324 Broadened 2 variants
The inculcation of civic and personal virtue requires guided instruction and monitoring rather than natural development.
The 1996 (SWN) focus on the instruction of 'virtue' is broadened in 2023 (SK) to encompass the acquisition of 'literacy' as a whole.
SK The acquisition of literacy requires emphatic cultural instruction rather than natural development.
SWN The inculcation of civic and personal virtue requires guided instruction and monitoring rather than natural development.
77879606101620222324 Broadened 2 variants
The American transnational idea is based on universality, contrasting with the European idea of a nation based on descent or 'blood and soil.'

SK The modern nation is not based on race or descent.
MoA The American transnational idea is based on universality, contrasting with the European idea of a nation based on des...
77879606101620222324 Evolved 2 variants
The conveyance of shared knowledge to children is a fundamental fact of linguistic communication, not an ideological choice.
