SK The conveyance of shared knowledge to children is a fundamental fact of linguistic communication, not an ideological ...
PoC Linguistic communication is an ongoing process of uncertainty reduction.
77879606101620222324 Evolved 2 variants
A sense of community between the rich and poor in the United States has declined, giving way to an us-versus-them mentality even regarding the welfare of children.

SK United States elementary schooling has declined to twenty-fifth in the world.
SWN A sense of community between the rich and poor in the United States has declined, giving way to an us-versus-them men...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants

77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants

International groupings like the European Union have not replaced the nation-state and are not destined to do so.

SK The nation-state is not being replaced by globalism.
HtEC International groupings like the European Union have not replaced the nation-state and are not destined to do so.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Administrators and professors in teacher-training institutes are the primary defenders of the status-quo, child-centered ideas that dominate schools.

77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants

AE Poor children receive ineffective instruction in both their home and school environments.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants

SK Increasing the quantity of knowledge conveyed to all students simultaneously promotes both learning and equality.

77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants

RE Readability scores based on quantifiable elements like sentence length and word frequency are inadequate for determin...
77879606101620222324 Evolved 2 variants
Irony is a common feature of everyday language in both rural and urban settings, not an elite phenomenon.
The focus shifts from the temporal nature of language processing in PoC (1977) to the social distribution of irony in MoA (2010).
PoC Language processing is a temporal phenomenon.
MoA Irony is a common feature of everyday language in both rural and urban settings, not an elite phenomenon.
77879606101620222324 Evolved 2 variants
The primary goal of inquiry-based instruction is to develop student independence and teach children how to learn for themselves using the scientific method.
The emphasis moves from students internalizing editorial principles in writing in PoC (1977) to developing scientific independence through inquiry in SWN (1996).

SWN The primary goal of inquiry-based instruction is to develop student independence and teach children how to learn for ...
77879606101620222324 Broadened 2 variants
Cooperative learning is an excellent method of instruction only when used in conjunction with whole-class instruction.
The specific technique of in-class revision from PoC (1977) is generalized into a broader discussion of cooperative versus whole-class instruction in SWN (1996).
PoC Publicly revising a text in class is an efficient method of instruction.
SWN Cooperative learning is an excellent method of instruction only when used in conjunction with whole-class instruction.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Reliable tests of writing proficiency are a prerequisite for serious progress in teaching, research, and objective evaluation of students and teachers.
The call for reliable writing tests in PoC (1977) is refined in SWN (1996) to emphasize their role as psychological achievement incentives.
PoC Reliable tests of writing proficiency are a prerequisite for serious progress in teaching, research, and objective ev...
SWN Objective tests function as achievement incentives for students and teachers.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
National focus should not be diverted by disputes over the details of cultural inclusion away from the primary goal of improving literacy-competence and equality.
The argument against multi-cultural focus in CL (1987) is refined in AE (2022) to emphasize that such disputes distract from literacy and equality.
CL Multi-cultural education should not be the primary focus of national education.
AE National focus should not be diverted by disputes over the details of cultural inclusion away from the primary goal o...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Reading does not follow an orderly, linear pattern of identifying words, then meanings, then sentences, and finally text meanings.
The author consistently rejects the linear 'bottom-up' model of reading across CL (1987) and AE (2022).
CL Reading does not follow an orderly, linear pattern of identifying words, then meanings, then sentences, and finally t...
AE Reading does not follow an orderly, bottom-up pattern of identifying words, then meanings, then sentences, then whole...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
The reader's mind is not just a decoder of written text but an active supplier of essential information that is not written down.
The reader's role as an information supplier in CL (1987) is specified as a supplier of 'cultural' information in AE (2022).
CL The reader's mind is not just a decoder of written text but an active supplier of essential information that is not w...
AE The reader acts as a supplier of essential cultural information that is not explicitly written in the text.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants

AE Failure to perform a task reliably occurs whenever the task consistently forces the individual to exceed the capacity...

The observation that surface form is lost while meaning is retained is identical in CL (1987) and AE (2022).

AE The original form of a sentence is rapidly lost to memory, whereas an accurate memory for its meaning is retained.

AE Inferences regarding sentence meaning are made at the time a sentence is understood, not later during recognition or ...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Accurate memory of surface form normally disappears in a few seconds, while accurate memory for gist is quite persistent.

77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants

77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants

CL All human experience is ongoing, partial, and fragmented.
AE Cognitive experience is inherently partial and fragmented, requiring past knowledge to interpret momentary sensory da...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants

The observation about middle-level categories in CL (1987) is updated with the more formal 'basic-level' terminology in AE (2022).
CL Children learn middle-level categories first in acquiring language, learning terms like 'tree' before 'oak' and 'dog'...
AE Children naturally learn 'basic-level' terms such as 'tree' or 'dog' before they acquire more specific terms like 'oa...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Middle-level categories, or basic-level terms, constitute the 'basic furniture' of the human conceptual world.
The 'basic furniture' metaphor for middle-level categories is preserved across 35 years between CL (1987) and AE (2022).
CL Middle-level categories, or basic-level terms, constitute the 'basic furniture' of the human conceptual world.
AE Middle-level categories serve as the fundamental framework or 'basic furniture' of the human conceptual world.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
People within a culture exhibit high levels of consistency regarding which specific examples are 'typical' members of a category.
The claim regarding cultural consensus on category prototypes is consistently presented in CL (1987) and AE (2022).
CL Members of a specific culture consistently agree on which examples are most typical of a category.
AE People within a culture exhibit high levels of consistency regarding which specific examples are 'typical' members of...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Sentences using a general class name are perceived as making sense only if the specific member substituted for that class name matches the typical behavior of the class prototype.
The logic of substituting class names in CL (1987) is refined in AE (2022) to explicitly reference 'class prototypes' and 'typical behavior.'
CL Sentences using a general class name only remain sensible when a typical member of that class is substituted.
AE Sentences using a general class name are perceived as making sense only if the specific member substituted for that c...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Human beings make sense of present experiences by assimilating them to prototypes formed from past experiences.
The core thesis that present experiences are understood via assimilation to past prototypes is repeated in CL (1987) and AE (2022).
CL Current experiences are rendered meaningful by assimilating them to prototypes formed from past experiences.
AE Human beings make sense of present experiences by assimilating them to prototypes formed from past experiences.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Mental prototypes are better described as 'schemata'—abstract mental entities—than as concrete images.
The definition of schemata as abstract mental entities rather than concrete images remains consistent across the 35-year gap.
CL Schemata are abstract mental entities rather than concrete images.
AE Mental prototypes are better described as 'schemata'—abstract mental entities—than as concrete images.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
The human mind possesses a remarkable ability to adjust initial schemata unconsciously based on situational factors.
The author maintains the same claim about the mind's ability to unconsciously adjust schemata/conceptions based on context.
CL The human mind possesses a remarkable ability to adjust initial schemata unconsciously based on situational factors.
AE The human mind possesses a remarkable ability to adjust initial conceptions unconsciously based on the situation.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
In the process of reading, readers adjust their initial schemata to fit the specific details of the text.
The specific application of schema adjustment to the process of reading is restated with only minor stylistic variations.
CL In the process of reading, readers adjust their initial schemata to fit the specific details of the text.
AE In reading, individuals adjust their initial schemata to the specific requirements of the text.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Schemata perform two essential functions for literacy: storing knowledge in retrievable form and organizing it for efficient application.
The dual functions of schemata—storage and efficient deployment/application—are preserved identically in both texts.
CL Schemata serve two essential functions for literacy: storing knowledge in retrievable form and organizing it for effi...
AE Schemata perform two essential functions for literacy: storing knowledge in retrievable form and organizing it for ef...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Slowness of reading beyond a certain point makes assimilation of complex meaning impossible.
The argument that a minimum reading speed is required for complex meaning assimilation is repeated with near-identical phrasing.
CL Reading slowness beyond a certain threshold prevents the assimilation of complex meaning.
AE Slowness of reading beyond a certain point makes assimilation of complex meaning impossible.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Literacy requires both intensive knowledge of relationships and extensive knowledge of specifics.
The assertion that literacy necessitates both intensive relational knowledge and extensive specific knowledge is unchanged.
CL Literacy requires both intensive knowledge of relationships and extensive knowledge of specifics.
AE Literacy requires both intensive knowledge of relationships and extensive knowledge of specifics.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Dictionaries and encyclopedias are impractical tools for comprehension if a reader lacks crucial, quickly available background information.
The argument regarding the limited utility of reference tools for readers lacking background knowledge remains the same.
CL Dictionaries and encyclopedias are impractical tools for comprehension if a reader lacks crucial, quickly available b...
AE Dictionaries and encyclopedias are impractical tools for comprehension if a reader constantly lacks crucial backgroun...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
A consistent lack of necessary information can make the reading process so laborious and uncommunicative that it fails to convey meaning.
The author adds nuance in 2022 by describing the failed reading process as not just laborious but also 'uncommunicative.'
CL A consistent lack of necessary information can make the reading process so laborious that it fails to convey meaning.
AE A consistent lack of necessary information can make the reading process so laborious and uncommunicative that it fail...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Experts perform better than novices not because of better intellectual machinery, but because they have more relevant and quickly available information.
The core thesis that expert performance is driven by information access rather than innate 'machinery' is perfectly preserved.
CL Experts perform better than novices because they have more relevant and quickly available information, not more power...
AE Experts perform better than novices not because of better intellectual machinery, but because they have more relevant...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Good readers are distinguished from poor ones simply by the possession of a lot of diverse, task-specific information.
The distinction between good and poor readers as a matter of task-specific information possession remains the central argument.
CL Good readers are distinguished from poor ones simply by the possession of a lot of diverse, task-specific information.
AE The primary distinction between good and poor readers is the possession of diverse, task-specific information.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Expert performance in complex domains depends on the quick deployment of mental schemata.
The argument is slightly narrowed to focus on expert performance specifically within 'complex domains.'
CL Expert performance depends on the quick deployment of stored schemata.
AE Expert performance in complex domains depends on the quick deployment of mental schemata.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Good readers avoid short-term memory overload by ignoring small-scale textual features in favor of overall structure.
The claim that good readers selectively ignore small-scale features to preserve short-term memory remains constant.
CL Good readers ignore small-scale features of a text to free up short-term memory for overall structural meaning.
AE Good readers avoid short-term memory overload by ignoring small-scale textual features in favor of overall structure.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Unskilled readers experience memory overload because they lack a store of relevant schemata and must process small-scale meaning relationships individually.
The later text clarifies that unskilled readers process relationships 'individually' because they lack a stored 'store' of schemata.
CL Unskilled readers experience memory overload because they must process small-scale meaning relationships due to a lac...
AE Unskilled readers experience memory overload because they lack a store of relevant schemata and must process small-sc...
77879606101620222324 Broadened 2 variants
Unlike chess schemata, verbal schemata must be shared across the language community to enable communication.
The 1987 claim about verbal schemata is generalized in 2020 to encompass 'knowledge' in the context of both language and 'culture.'
CL Unlike chess schemata, verbal schemata must be shared across the language community to enable communication.
HtEC Knowledge must be shared across the community to be functional in language and culture.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Even ninth-grade students fail to attain the perfect accuracy displayed by adults on initial trials of complex communication tasks.
The observation that ninth-graders struggle with initial trials of complex communication compared to adults is repeated.
CL Even ninth-graders fail to reach adult levels of accuracy on the first trial of complex communication tasks.
AE Even ninth-grade students fail to attain the perfect accuracy displayed by adults on initial trials of complex commun...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
A lack of shared information strains cognitive capacities when individuals attempt to carry out communicative tasks.
The argument is refined by specifying that a lack of 'shared' information is what causes cognitive strain during communication.
CL A lack of information strains cognitive capacities when individuals attempt to carry out communication tasks.
AE A lack of shared information strains cognitive capacities when individuals attempt to carry out communicative tasks.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Children communicate successfully and engage in social, nonegocentric speech when the demands of the task are relatively light.
The claim regarding children's ability to engage in nonegocentric speech under light task loads is maintained.
CL Children engage in successful, social, nonegocentric speech when the demands of the task are relatively light.
AE Children communicate successfully and engage in social, nonegocentric speech when the demands of the task are relativ...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Effective reading requires the reader to monitor whether their personal schematic associations are appropriate and shared by the wider speech community.
The requirement for readers to monitor the appropriateness of their schematic associations against the community remains the same.
CL Effective reading requires the reader to monitor whether their personal schematic associations are appropriate and sh...
AE Effective reading requires the reader to monitor whether their personal schematic associations are shared by the wide...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Literate adults have internalized shared schemata to the point that they function as 'second nature.'
The concept of shared schemata becoming 'second nature' for literate adults is preserved without change.
CL Literate adults have internalized shared schemata to the point that they function as 'second nature.'
AE Literate adults have internalized shared schemata to the point that they function as second nature.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Semi-literates and young children lack readily accessible information regarding what other members of the speech community can be expected to know.
The later text specifies that what is missing for semi-literates is knowledge of what other 'members of the speech community' know.
CL Semiliterates and young children lack readily accessible information regarding what information is shared by others.
AE Semi-literates and young children lack readily accessible information regarding what other members of the speech comm...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Cultural knowledge of shared associations is not strictly a function of chronological age.
The author updates 'cultural knowledge' to 'ethnic knowledge' and 'chronological' to 'biological' age, emphasizing communicative stores.
CL Cultural knowledge of shared associations is not strictly a function of chronological age.
AE Ethnic knowledge—or the communicative store—is not strictly a function of biological age.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Standardized national languages were fixed by seventeenth-, eighteenth-, and nineteenth-century normalizers often acting under the direction of central governments.
The 2022 text adds historical precision by specifying the 'seventeenth-, eighteenth-, and nineteenth-century' timeframe for language normalization.
CL Standardized national languages were fixed by language normalizers acting under the direction of central national gov...
AE Standardized national languages were fixed by seventeenth-, eighteenth-, and nineteenth-century normalizers often act...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
In agrarian societies, local oral dialects were sufficient because economic units were small and community positions were fixed.
The argument is refined by framing the isolation of agrarian dialects as a lack of 'handicap' due to local economic fixes.
CL In agrarian societies, local oral dialects were sufficient because economic units were small and community positions ...
AE In agrarian societies, the mutual unintelligibility of dialects was not a handicap because economic units were local ...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
National education systems teaching literacy and common culture are the core mechanism of modern nationhood.
The later version clarifies that national education systems specifically teach 'common ethnicity' alongside literacy.
CL National education systems teaching literacy and common culture are the core mechanism of modern nationhood.
AE National systems of education teach common ethnicity and literacy as the core of modern nationhood.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Members of industrial society must be able to communicate using written, impersonal, context-free messages in a shared linguistic medium.
The requirement for context-free communication in industrial society is refined by adding the need for a 'shared linguistic medium.'
CL Members of industrial society must be able to communicate via written, impersonal, context-free messages.
AE Members of industrial society must be able to communicate using written, impersonal, context-free messages in a share...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Universal literacy and high levels of numerical, technical, and general sophistication are functional prerequisites for industrial society.
The 2022 text explicitly adds 'universal literacy' to the list of functional prerequisites for industrial society.
CL Numerical, technical, and general sophistication are functional prerequisites for industrial society.
AE Universal literacy and high levels of numerical, technical, and general sophistication are functional prerequisites f...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
The necessity of a standardized language for modern economies explains why expensive systems of universal education are found in all modern nations, including totalitarian ones.
The later text provides a causal explanation, linking standardized language needs to the presence of education systems even in totalitarian states.
CL Universal systems of education are a universal feature of modern nations because of the weight of their social conseq...
AE The necessity of a standardized language for modern economies explains why expensive systems of universal education a...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
The national vocabulary of a literate person consists of three distinct domains: international, trans-national language-based, and country-specific.
The author maintains a consistent three-part taxonomy for national vocabulary across 35 years, with only minor terminological updates (CL 1987, AE 2022).
CL Modern national vocabulary consists of three distinct domains: international, transnational (language-specific), and ...
AE The national vocabulary of a literate person consists of three distinct domains: international, trans-national langua...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
International literacy requires a core lexicon of world history, geography, math, and science that is taught across all national educational systems.
The general concept of a core lexicon in CL (1987) is refined in AE (2022) to specify the exact academic disciplines required for international literacy.
CL The international core lexicon of modern education is taught in all national educational systems regardless of language.
AE International literacy requires a core lexicon of world history, geography, math, and science that is taught across a...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Traditional Anglo-centric cultural elements are likely to remain central to global English literacy because they provide a useful basis for international exchange.
The argument that Anglo-centric cultural elements persist due to their utility in international exchange remains unchanged between CL (1987) and AE (2022).
CL Traditional elements of English cultural literacy persist because they facilitate international exchange.
AE Traditional Anglo-centric cultural elements are likely to remain central to global English literacy because they prov...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Effective standardization of spoken language is possible without alphabetic script if a national education system enforces common standards for pronunciation and grammar.
The broad claim about non-alphabetic standardization in CL (1987) is made more precise in AE (2022) by specifying enforcement of pronunciation and grammar.
CL Effective standardization of spoken language is possible without an alphabetic script if a national education system ...
AE Effective standardization of spoken language is possible without alphabetic script if a national education system enf...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Court Castilian was established as the dialect base of the Spanish national language and its dictionary remains the basis for instruction wherever Spanish is spoken.
The author expands the scope of the claim from Spanish instruction generally in CL (1987) to its global dictionary-based application in AE (2022).
CL The Real Academia de la Lengua established court Castilian as the permanent basis for Spanish instruction.
AE Court Castilian was established as the dialect base of the Spanish national language and its dictionary remains the b...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
English language standardization was achieved through indirect means, primarily dictionaries and grammars, rather than a formal national academy.
The abstract 'intellectual influence' cited in CL (1987) is specifically identified as the influence of dictionaries and grammars in AE (2022).
CL English standardization was achieved through indirect intellectual influence rather than a formal national academy.
AE English language standardization was achieved through indirect means, primarily dictionaries and grammars, rather tha...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Language standardization is characterized by initial arbitrariness, similar to the setting of scientific standards like the meter.
The general comparison to measurement standards in CL (1987) is refined to a specific scientific example, the meter, in AE (2022).
CL Language standardization is characterized by an initial arbitrariness similar to the creation of physical measurement...
AE Language standardization is characterized by initial arbitrariness, similar to the setting of scientific standards li...
77879606101620222324 Evolved 2 variants
The story of the cherry tree serves as a useful illustration of the processes by which American national culture was formed.
The author moves from a specific cultural myth in CL (1987) to a broader sociological observation about 'communitarian spirit' in SWN (1996).
CL The story of the cherry tree serves as a useful illustration of the processes by which American national culture was ...
SWN The 'communitarian spirit' was previously a defining characteristic of American national culture.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
The development of large-scale social and ethnic unification was a process whose underlying causes were often obscure to the societies that produced them.
The thesis regarding the obscurity of the causes of social unification is repeated almost verbatim between CL (1987) and AE (2022).
CL The underlying causes of social and cultural unification were often obscure even to the historical societies that wer...
AE The development of large-scale social and ethnic unification was a process whose underlying causes were often obscure...
77879606101620222324 Evolved 2 variants
The history of American secondary education since the 1920s is characterized by the dominance of the 'Cardinal Principles' over the humanistic 'Committee of Ten' principles.
The technical historical critique of educational principles in CL (1987) shifts to a broader polemic regarding institutional 'illusion' in SWN (1996).
CL The history of American secondary education since the 1920s is characterized by the dominance of the 'Cardinal Princi...
SWN The history of American education since the 1930s is defined by the persistence of illusion in the face of reality.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
The 1918 Cardinal Principles report successfully shifted the focus of American schooling toward nonacademic goals like health, vocation, and leisure.
The author adds specific nonacademic categories like health and vocation in SWN (1996) to the general 'needs of the child' framing from CL (1987).
CL The 1918 Cardinal Principles report shifted the focus of American schools toward the needs of the child and society, ...
SWN The 1918 Cardinal Principles report successfully shifted the focus of American schooling toward nonacademic goals lik...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
A people is best unified by teaching children the best items of their intellectual and moral heritage.
The core belief that childhood instruction in moral heritage is the primary source of national unity is repeated from CL (1987) to HtEC (2020).
CL A people is best unified by being taught their intellectual and moral heritage during childhood.
HtEC A people is best unified by teaching children the best items of their intellectual and moral heritage.
77879606101620222324 Evolved 2 variants
The goal for framing educational theories that guide policy should be independent data convergence, where multiple independent methods yield similar results.
The focus shifts from the personal goal of 'mature literacy' in CL (1987) to a methodological goal of 'data convergence' in KD (2006).
CL The educational goal for all citizens should be mature literacy.
KD The goal for framing educational theories that guide policy should be independent data convergence, where multiple in...
77879606101620222324 Evolved 2 variants
In the 1940s, American K–12 education ranked among the best and fairest school systems in the world.
The author's assessment of American K-12 quality shifts from a contemporary failure in SWN (1996) to a historical defense of its 1940s efficacy in WKM (2016).
SWN American K-12 education is among the least effective in the developed world.
WKM In the 1940s, American K–12 education ranked among the best and fairest school systems in the world.
77879606101620222324 Narrowed 2 variants
The progressive claim that a child's interest in a subject derives solely from its connection with their immediate experiences and home surroundings is false.
The broad critique of progressive pedagogical theory in SWN (1996) is narrowed to a specific observation about linguistic differences in KD (2006).
SWN The progressive claim that a child's interest in a subject derives solely from its connection with their immediate ex...
KD The language used in reading and writing is often different from the language spoken in a child's home.
77879606101620222324 Evolved 2 variants
The primary obstacle to educational reform is a controlling system of ideas, not the individuals working in schools.
The obstacle to reform shifts from a system of abstract ideas in SWN (1996) to the specific lack of a shared 'speech community' in AE (2022).
SWN The primary obstacle to educational reform is a controlling system of ideas, not the individuals working in schools.
AE The absence of a shared 'speech community' in the classroom is a primary obstacle to achieving educational equality.
77879606101620222324 Evolved 2 variants
The fall of communism was primarily a failure of a socioeconomic theory that did not accord with reality, rather than merely a failure of a political arrangement.
The author applies the framing of 'theories not according with reality' from the fall of communism in SWN (1996) to modern diversity discourse in AE (2022).
SWN The fall of communism was primarily a failure of a socioeconomic theory that did not accord with reality, rather than...
AE The conceptualization of diversity as a dominant rather than subordinate good is a theory that does not accord with r...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Shared frames of reference must be the common property of all people to escape avoidable differences in competence that result in social inequality.
The necessity of shared reference frames to prevent social inequality is restated with nearly identical phrasing between SWN (1996) and RE (2024).
SWN Shared frames of reference are necessary common property to prevent social inequality resulting from differences in c...
RE Shared frames of reference must be the common property of all people to escape avoidable differences in competence th...
77879606101620222324 Narrowed 2 variants
The United States has one of the highest mobility rates of all developed countries, with approximately one-fifth of all Americans relocating annually.
The broad claim about general American population mobility in SWN (1996) is narrowed to specifically address school mobility in KD (2006).
SWN The United States has one of the highest mobility rates of all developed countries, with approximately one-fifth of a...
KD The United States possesses the highest rate of school mobility among all developed nations.
77879606101620222324 Narrowed 2 variants
The concept of the 'local school' is a myth in urban environments because the student population is too unstable to constitute a stable community.
The general myth of the 'local school' in SWN (1996) is specifically contextualized to urban environments in KD (2006).
SWN The concept of a 'local school' is a myth if it implies a stable population of students attending throughout the year.
KD The concept of the 'local school' is a myth in urban environments because the student population is too unstable to c...
77879606101620222324 New Evidence 2 variants
Jean Piaget's theories are currently used by educators to justify the delay of academic content based on 'developmental inappropriateness.'
The critique of 'developmental appropriateness' in SWN (1996) is explicitly linked to Piagetian theory in WKM (2016) as the underlying justification.
SWN The concept of 'developmental appropriateness' is used by educators to justify withholding academic content from youn...
WKM Jean Piaget's theories are currently used by educators to justify the delay of academic content based on 'development...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
The shift from McGuffey Readers to modern stories in early childhood curriculum represents a move away from moral training toward a focus on imagination.
The general shift from McGuffey readers in SWN (1996) is refined in RE (2024) by attributing the change specifically to the influence of Romanticism.
SWN The shift from McGuffey Readers to modern stories in early childhood curriculum represents a move away from moral tra...
RE The shift to romanticism caused early-grade curriculum to move from explicit moral tales to a focus on fairy tales an...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Starting preschool at age two can enable a disadvantaged child to catch up with highly advantaged children by age ten if followed by a knowledge-based primary school.
The author refines the timeline and conditions for a disadvantaged child's academic recovery between SWN (1996) and WKM (2016).
SWN Disadvantaged children who attend academic preschool at age two can reach academic parity with highly advantaged chil...
WKM Starting preschool at age two can enable a disadvantaged child to catch up with highly advantaged children by age ten...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Modern theories of learning styles and multiple intelligences are merely updated versions of the long-standing individualistic principle in American education.
The claim that learning styles and multiple intelligences are versions of Romantic individualism is repeated from SWN (1996) to WKM (2016).
SWN The 'individual learning styles' and 'multiple intelligences' movements are modern, scientized versions of Romantic i...
WKM Modern theories of learning styles and multiple intelligences are merely updated versions of the long-standing indivi...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
The educational claim that students need only learn 'accessing skills' (how to look things up) is bankrupt because it can reinforce misconceptions with spurious exactitude.
The critique of 'accessing skills' in SWN (1996) is sharpened into a specific scientific rejection of the 'Google' excuse in HtEC (2020).
SWN The educational claim that students need only learn 'accessing skills' (how to look things up) is bankrupt because it...
HtEC The claim that students do not need to learn facts because they can look them up on Google is scientifically false.
77879606101620222324 Narrowed 2 variants
Children possess an innate faculty for oral language but lack an innate faculty for alphabetic learning.
A broad claim about an innate learning instinct in SWN (1996) is narrowed in KD (2006) to distinguish between innate oral language and non-innate alphabetic learning.
SWN Human children possess an innate learning instinct necessary for survival.
KD Children possess an innate faculty for oral language but lack an innate faculty for alphabetic learning.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Current educational research is characterized by high variability in quality and applicability.
The author consistently notes the uneven quality and reliability of educational research between SWN (1996) and AE (2022).
SWN Educational research varies enormously in quality and reliability.
AE Current educational research is characterized by high variability in quality and applicability.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Attempting to provide individual tutorial attention in classrooms of twenty to thirty students results in individual neglect.
The logistical argument that individual attention in a group setting leads to neglect remains unchanged from SWN (1996) to WKM (2016).
SWN Attempting to provide individual tutorial attention in classrooms of twenty to thirty students results in individual ...
WKM Providing individual attention to some students in a school setting necessarily results in the neglect of others.
77879606101620222324 Evolved 2 variants
The conflict between the goals of No Child Left Behind (NCLB) and the naturalist/formalist ideas of schools is the root cause of the current educational impasse.
A 2006 claim about an impasse between naturalist ideas and policy goals shifts in AE (2022) to argue there is no inherent conflict between literacy and self-esteem.
KD The conflict between the goals of No Child Left Behind (NCLB) and the naturalist/formalist ideas of schools is the ro...
AE There is no conflict between the goals of achieving literacy and fostering self-esteem.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
A student must typically know approximately 90 percent of the words in a passage to comprehend it and learn new vocabulary from it.
The 90 percent word comprehension threshold mentioned in KD (2006) is refined in MoA (2010) to include its role in learning new vocabulary.

MoA A student must typically know approximately 90 percent of the words in a passage to comprehend it and learn new vocab...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Any academic topic can be taught to any child in an appropriate mode if the teacher accounts for the student's current knowledge.
The 2006 claim about teaching any topic at any age is refined in 2016 to specifically emphasize accounting for the student's prior knowledge.
KD Almost any academic topic can be taught at any school age if it is taught in an appropriate manner.
WKM Any academic topic can be taught to any child in an appropriate mode if the teacher accounts for the student's curren...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
The American founders believed internal factions—based on ethnicity, region, religion, and class—posed a mortal danger to the republic.
The 2010 warning about internal factions is refined in HtEC (2020) by attributing this view to the American founders and detailing the types of factions.
MoA Internal factions represent the most significant 'mortal danger' to the American Republic.
HtEC The American founders believed internal factions—based on ethnicity, region, religion, and class—posed a mortal dange...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Constitutional checks and balances are insufficient to preserve a republic without a citizenry that subordinates local interests to the common good.
The 2010 claim about the insufficiency of checks and balances is refined in 2020 to specify that a citizenry focused on the common good is the missing requirement.
MoA The structural checks and balances of the national government are insufficient on their own to preserve the Republic.
HtEC Constitutional checks and balances are insufficient to preserve a republic without a citizenry that subordinates loca...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
The school serves as the primary institution for transforming future citizens into loyal Americans by teaching common knowledge, virtues, and language.
The author maintains the core view that schools are the primary vehicles for national identity and virtue across MoA (2010) and HtEC (2020).
MoA The school serves as the primary institution for transforming future citizens into loyal Americans by teaching common...
HtEC Schools were intended to be the primary institution for transforming future citizens into loyal Americans by teaching...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
The success or failure of the American experiment in self-government is wholly dependent on the cultivation of the minds of the young in schools.
The existential link between self-government and the cultivation of young minds is stated nearly identically in MoA (2010) and HtEC (2020).
MoA The success or failure of the American experiment in self-government is wholly dependent on the cultivation of the mi...
HtEC The success or failure of self-government is wholly dependent on the cultivation of the minds of the young in schools.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
The American Union must be sustained by the moral and intellectual powers of the community because physical force cannot produce lasting union.
The preference for moral/intellectual power over physical force to sustain the Union is repeated from MoA (2010) to HtEC (2020).
MoA The American Union must be sustained by the moral and intellectual powers of the community because physical force can...
HtEC The American Union must be sustained by the moral and intellectual powers of the community rather than physical force.
77879606101620222324 Broadened 2 variants
American education in the 19th century was more successful than European education because it directed students toward public political life rather than private life.
A 2010 comparison of American and European educational directions is broadened in 2020 into a historical claim about the relative success of the American model.
MoA Education in the United States is historically directed toward political life, whereas European education was histori...
HtEC American education in the 19th century was more successful than European education because it directed students towar...
77879606101620222324 Narrowed 2 variants
Ethnicity is an artificially constructed identity based on language and school-promulgated knowledge rather than something innate or racial.
A complex 2010 definition of ethnicity involving school-promulgated knowledge is narrowed in AE (2022) to focus primarily on language standardization.
MoA Ethnicity is an artificially constructed identity based on language and school-promulgated knowledge rather than some...
AE Modern ethnicity is fundamentally based on language standardization.
77879606101620222324 Broadened 2 variants
Education has the power to enlarge the cultivated class and obliterate factitious social distinctions between property and labor.
A 2010 claim about education's ability to erase social class distinctions is broadened in 2020 to its general power to control human impulses.
MoA Education has the power to enlarge the cultivated class and obliterate factitious social distinctions between propert...
HtEC Social education has the power to override and control conflicting human impulses.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
The overall performance of charter schools has not been significantly better than that of ordinary public schools.
The assessment that charter schools do not significantly outperform public schools remains unchanged between MoA (2010) and WKM (2016).
MoA The overall performance of charter schools has not been significantly better than that of ordinary public schools.
WKM Charter schools' overall results are not significantly better than those of regular public schools.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Belief systems should be excluded from the public sphere and confined to the private sphere to pacify conflicts of power and interests.
The 2010 argument for excluding private beliefs from the public sphere is refined in RE (2024) to target 'Romanticism' specifically within educational policy.
MoA Belief systems should be excluded from the public sphere and confined to the private sphere to pacify conflicts of po...
RE Romanticism should be restricted to the domain of poetry and excluded from the domain of educational policy.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
George Washington viewed the systematic dissemination of learning as a tool to eliminate local attachments and state prejudices in favor of public obligation.
The historical analysis of Washington's views on education as a tool against localism is consistent in MoA (2010) and HtEC (2020).
MoA George Washington viewed the systematic dissemination of learning as a tool to eliminate local attachments and state ...
HtEC George Washington intended for education to spread systematic ideas across the nation to dissolve local attachments a...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Doctrines taught in many schools of education are at odds with the findings of other university departments, particularly cognitive psychology.
The 2010 observation about inter-departmental conflict is refined in 2020 to specifically contrast education school doctrines with cognitive psychology.
MoA University psychology departments are generally critical of the 'ideological psychology' taught in colleges of educat...
HtEC Doctrines taught in many schools of education are at odds with the findings of other university departments, particul...
77879606101620222324 Broadened 2 variants
The French school system's detailed records allow for the observation of educational reforms with the decisiveness of a laboratory experiment.
The 2016 description of a specific French reform is broadened in 2022 to a general methodological point about using the French system as a laboratory.
WKM The 1989 French educational reform constituted a complete 'Americanization' of the French school system.
AE The French school system's detailed records allow for the observation of educational reforms with the decisiveness of...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
The substance of what is learned in grades K–5 determines whether disadvantaged students will catch up and whether students will be ready for high school and college.
The core claim that K-5 education is the primary determinant for the success of disadvantaged students remains consistent between WKM (2016) and SK (2023).
WKM The substance of what is learned in grades K–5 determines whether disadvantaged students will catch up and whether st...
SK The quality of education in grades K-5 is life-determining for disadvantaged children.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
The current American K-3 curriculum contains no mythology, legends, biographies, hero tales, or great historical events.
The author maintains the exact same list of missing elements (mythology, legends, biographies, hero tales) in the K-3 curriculum across both books.
WKM The current American K-3 curriculum contains no mythology, legends, biographies, hero tales, or great historical events.
HtEC The current K-3 curriculum excludes essential mythology, legends, biographies, and heroic tales of the nation.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Reading aloud to children and classroom discussion should play a big role in the early grades.
The recommendation for specific classroom activities in WKM (2016) evolves into a broader call for a coherent general knowledge curriculum in RE (2024).
WKM Reading aloud to children and classroom discussion should play a big role in the early grades.
RE General knowledge should play a larger, more coherent role in the early grades.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
The belief that only specific demographic groups can or should know certain classic information is a form of 'cultural essentialism.'
The definition of 'cultural essentialism' is clarified from a general tribalist threat in WKM (2016) to a specific demographic restriction on knowledge in AE (2022).
WKM Cultural essentialism is a form of tribalism that contradicts the aims of the American experiment.
AE The belief that only specific demographic groups can or should know certain classic information is a form of 'cultura...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
The decline of the French school system was caused by avoidable intellectual mistakes made by top intellectuals in 1989.
The author consistently attributes the decline of French schools to specific intellectual errors made in 1989.
WKM The decline of the French school system was caused by avoidable intellectual mistakes made by top intellectuals in 1989.
RE French intellectuals made significant and avoidable intellectual mistakes in 1989 that damaged the school system.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
France's adoption of skill-based, individualized educational principles caused it to decline from one of the best and most equitable systems to one of the worst and least equitable.
The claim that France moved from the 'best and most equitable' to 'worst and least equitable' due to skill-based principles is repeated across both texts.
WKM France's adoption of skill-based, individualized educational principles caused it to decline from one of the best and...
RE France's educational system declined from being one of the best and most equitable in the world to one of the worst a...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
The idea-system of 'providential individualism' acts like a virus that prevents educational improvement across national boundaries.
The metaphorical framing of 'providential individualism' as a virus or infection remains consistent between 2016 and 2024.
WKM The idea-system of 'providential individualism' acts like a virus that prevents educational improvement across nation...
RE American schools are infected by a system of ideas called 'providential individualism' that prevents educational impr...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
American ten-year-olds are not fundamentally different from French ten-year-olds regarding their potential for math and reading proficiency.
The assertion that cognitive or proficiency potential is identical between American and French children is maintained across both books.
WKM American ten-year-olds are not fundamentally different from French ten-year-olds regarding cognitive proficiency in m...
RE American ten-year-olds are not fundamentally different from French ten-year-olds regarding their potential for math a...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
France's success in overcoming inequality prior to 1989 was due to its excellent preschool system and its highly communal primary schools with a common curriculum.
The attribution of France's historical success to preschools and a common curriculum is repeated with near-identical wording.
WKM France's earlier success in overcoming inequality was due to universal preschool attendance and a communal primary sc...
RE France's success in overcoming inequality prior to 1989 was due to its excellent preschool system and its highly comm...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
The traditional aim of French schooling from the 18th century until 1977 was to provide all children the same knowledge to unify society and reduce inequality.
The description of the historical French educational goal as unifying society through shared knowledge remains consistent.
WKM The traditional aim of French schooling from the 18th century until 1977 was to provide all children the same knowled...
RE The basic idea of French schooling in 1977 was to enable all children to acquire the same knowledge to unify society ...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
The Jospin law established twenty-eight new education colleges across France's autonomous districts specifically to codify and teach progressive pedagogical theory.
WKM (2016) mentions the creation of education colleges, while RE (2024) adds the detail of the Jospin law and their specific autonomous districts.
WKM The creation of twenty-eight new education colleges in 1989 served to institutionalize and codify progressive pedagog...
RE The Jospin law established twenty-eight new education colleges across France's autonomous districts specifically to c...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
The national standards produced under the 1989 law (the 'socle') were too vague and nonspecific to effectively guide instruction.
The critique of the 1989 French 'socle' as being too vague and nonspecific to guide teachers is maintained.
WKM The national standards produced under the 1989 law (the 'socle') were too vague and nonspecific to effectively guide ...
RE The 1989 French national standards (the 'socle') were too short and nonspecific to be practically useful.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
The French Ministry of Education continues to instruct schools to prioritize the plurality and diversity of student aptitudes despite years of educational decline.
The author continues to critique the French Ministry for prioritizing student diversity over curriculum despite poor results.
WKM The French Ministry of Education continues to instruct schools to prioritize the plurality and diversity of student a...
RE The French Ministry of Education continues to instruct schools to prioritize individual student diversity despite yea...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
The 1989 law granted students the right to elaborate their own curricula according to their own aspirations and capacities.
The specific point that the 1989 law allowed students to define their own curricula is repeated across both works.
WKM The 1989 law granted students the right to elaborate their own curricula according to their own aspirations and capac...
RE The 1989 law allowed French students to elaborate their own curricula based on their personal aspirations and capacit...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Progressive education in France encouraged naturalistic teaching of math and reading while deemphasizing memorization and grammar.
The description of French progressive education as prioritizing naturalism over memorization and grammar remains unchanged.
WKM Progressive education in France encouraged naturalistic teaching of math and reading while deemphasizing memorization...
RE Progressive education de-emphasizes memorization and grammar in favor of naturalistic teaching of math and reading.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
The rise in French primary students failing to read and reckon in the 1970s and 80s was a consequence of new progressive methods, not the old rigorous curriculum.
The author consistently argues that literacy failures in France were caused by new methods rather than the traditional curriculum.
WKM The rise in French primary students failing to read and reckon in the 1970s and 80s was a consequence of new progress...
RE The increase in French primary students failing to read and having to repeat grades was a consequence of new progress...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Naturalistic reading theories, such as 'la méthode globale,' caused basic reading difficulties in French pupils even before the national curriculum was officially changed.
RE (2024) adds 'la méthode mixte' to the critique of 'la méthode globale' found in WKM (2016) regarding reading difficulties.
WKM Naturalistic reading theories, such as 'la méthode globale,' caused basic reading difficulties in French pupils even ...
RE Naturalistic reading theories like 'la méthode globale' and 'la méthode mixte' caused basic reading difficulties in y...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
The failures caused by progressive methods were paradoxically used as evidence to argue for more progressive reforms and against the national curriculum.
The observation that progressive failures were used to justify further progressive reforms is a recurring argument.
WKM The failures caused by progressive methods were paradoxically used as evidence to argue for more progressive reforms ...
RE The rise in grade retentions caused by progressive methods was used as a rhetorical weapon against the old curriculum...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Pierre Bourdieu's 'The Inheritors' argued that French republican education was a mechanism for reproducing social class rather than overcoming it.
The summary of Bourdieu's critique of French education as a social reproduction mechanism remains consistent.
WKM Pierre Bourdieu argued that French republican education was a device for reproducing existing social stratifications ...
RE Pierre Bourdieu's 'The Inheritors' argued that French republican education was a mechanism for reproducing social cla...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants

The reference to Bourdieu's evidence regarding university majors and cultural tastes is maintained as a core part of the argument.
WKM Bourdieu's analysis of university majors and cultural tastes showed statistically significant correlations with socia...
RE Bourdieu's claim that equality of opportunity in the French system was a myth was based on class-based statistical di...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
The old French centralized system with a common curriculum was a product of the egalitarian left, designed to ensure equal opportunity.
The author consistently identifies the traditional French common curriculum as a project of the political left.
WKM The old French centralized system with a common curriculum was a product of the egalitarian left, designed to ensure ...
RE The old French centralized common curriculum was a product of egalitarian left-wing politics rather than right-wing c...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
The growth of knowledge does not render a broad, factual curriculum (encyclopedism) vain or obsolete.
The defense of 'encyclopedism' (broad factual knowledge) against claims of obsolescence is repeated across both years.
WKM The growth of knowledge does not render broad, 'encyclopedic' factual knowledge vain or obsolete.
RE The growth of knowledge does not render a broad, factual curriculum (encyclopedism) vain or obsolete.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
The intellectual origins of the French educational decline are the same as those that caused the American educational crisis: naturalism, individualism, and skill-centrism.
The author consistently links the educational crises in both France and the US to the same trio of ideological causes.
WKM The French educational 'debacle' was caused by the same theories—naturalism, individuality, and skill-centrism—that i...
RE The intellectual origins of the French educational decline are the same as those that caused the American educational...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
The data collected by the French Ministry of Education constitutes the most definitive comparative study of curriculum effects ever undertaken.
The 2024 text maintains the 2016 claim that French educational data represents the most definitive study of curriculum effects, with only minor phrasing adjustments.
WKM The French data from 1987 to 2007 constitute the most definitive comparative study of curriculum effects ever underta...
RE The data collected by the French Ministry of Education constitutes the most definitive comparative study of curriculu...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Teacher quality remained constant in France by all objective measures during the period of educational decline.
The assertion that teacher quality remained constant during France's educational decline is repeated with nearly identical wording in 2024.
WKM Teacher quality remained constant in France by all objective measures during the period of educational decline.
RE Teacher quality in France remained high and constant throughout the period of achievement decline.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Any US district implementing a communal, cumulative curriculum in all subjects will show higher achievement and equity than one under the skills-based paradigm.
The claim that US districts adopting communal curricula will see better outcomes remains a consistent prediction from 2016 to 2024.
WKM Any US district implementing a communal, cumulative curriculum in all subjects will show higher achievement and equit...
RE American school districts that implement a communal, cumulative curriculum in all subjects will see higher achievemen...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
The chief aim of the new French pedagogy was the imparting of all-purpose skills like 'learning to learn' rather than information.
The 2024 text updates the framing from the 'new' French pedagogy of 2016 to 'current' French educational theory.
WKM The chief aim of the new French pedagogy was the imparting of all-purpose skills like 'learning to learn' rather than...
RE The chief aim of current French educational theory is the imparting of all-purpose skills like 'learning to learn.'
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants

The preference for traditional methods like phonics over 'modern' child-centered methods is a repeated core argument across both years.

RE Students taught using traditional methods like phonics and dictation perform better than those taught using progressi...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants

The denial of cultural transmission in schools will eventually lead to social instability and violence.
The warning that denying cultural transmission leads to social violence is a repeated, verbatim core claim.
WKM The denial of cultural transmission in schools will eventually lead to social instability and violence.
RE The denial of cultural transmission in schools will eventually lead to social violence.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants

RE Educational decentralization in Sweden caused a severe drop in student abilities in core subjects.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants

The link between thinking-skills emphasis and the incoherence of the Swedish curriculum is a repeated argument from 2016 to 2024.

RE The Swedish elementary curriculum became incoherent due to its new emphasis on individuality and thinking skills.

RE The UK's Plowden Report (1967) institutionalized progressive, child-centered education as official left-wing doctrine...

AE High engagement with substantive academic content significantly reduces student behavioral problems and disciplinary ...

77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants

RE The belief that national competence increases as the population becomes more unified through shared communication was...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
What is 'appropriate' for a child to learn depends on what that child has already learned, not biological age alone.

RE What is 'appropriate' for a child to learn depends on what that child has already learned, not biological age alone.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants

77879606101620222324 Evolved 2 variants

SK A shared-knowledge curriculum increases the future earning power of students.

RE Current public schools lack coherent and common prior knowledge, preventing students from overcoming home-based disad...

SK Leaving content decisions up to individual teachers is a 'second-rate arrangement' and a source of unfairness.

77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
State legislators have a Constitutional duty to normalize grade-by-grade topic sequences in schools.

AE State legislators have a Constitutional duty to normalize grade-by-grade topic sequences in schools.
SK State legislators must mandate a minimal grade-by-grade core topic sequence in public schools.
77879606101620222324 New Evidence 2 variants

RE The United States ranks 25th among the nations of the world on PISA tests for reading, math, and science.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
The philosopher Hegel exercised a strong and permanent influence on John Dewey, who admitted that Hegelian thought left a 'permanent deposit' in his mind.
The 2024 book repeats the 2022 claim regarding Hegel's permanent influence on John Dewey using nearly identical phrasing.

RE John Dewey explicitly conceded that the philosophy of Hegel left a 'permanent deposit' in his mind.
77879606101620222324 Evolved 2 variants

77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants

RE The seedling metaphor for the human mind used by progressive education was explicitly rejected by Locke and Hume.

77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants

77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants

The 2024 version slightly simplifies the wording of the 2023 claim regarding the Common School's role in defining citizenship.
SK The original 'common school' ideal was centered on the idea that schools should prepare unified citizens to define th...
RE The original Common School vision was centered on the idea that schools define future citizens.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Incoherent elementary schooling leads to national incompetence and social injustice.
The 2024 text uses nearly identical language to the 2023 book to link incoherent schooling to national incompetence.
SK Incoherent elementary schooling fosters national incompetence and social injustice.
RE Incoherent elementary schooling leads to national incompetence and social injustice.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Educational incoherence fails to induce critical thinking because critical thinking is dependent on relevant knowledge.
The 2024 book maintains the 2023 position that critical thinking requires relevant knowledge and is hindered by educational incoherence.
SK Critical thinking is dependent on relevant knowledge and is not induced by educational incoherence.
RE Educational incoherence fails to induce critical thinking because critical thinking is dependent on relevant knowledge.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants

The 2024 text updates the terminology from 'scoundrels' to 'corrupt leaders' but preserves the core argument about voter ignorance.
SK Current pedagogical methods encourage ignorance that allows voters to be deceived and scoundrels to flourish.
RE Current educational methods encourage a level of ignorance that allows voters to be deceived and corrupt leaders to f...
77879606101620222324 Narrowed 2 variants
Radical change is unlikely to come from education schools because the child-centered tradition is too deeply ingrained.
The 2024 version narrows the scope from all 'education schools' to 'top university education schools.'
SK Radical change is unlikely to come from education schools because the child-centered tradition is too deeply ingrained.
RE Top university education schools are unlikely to reform because the child-centered tradition is too deeply ingrained.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Advantaged children start school with larger grapholectic vocabularies than disadvantaged children.
The claim regarding the vocabulary gap between advantaged and disadvantaged children remains virtually unchanged between 2023 and 2024.
SK Advantaged children start school with larger grapholectic vocabularies than disadvantaged children.
RE Advantaged children enter school with larger grapholectal vocabularies than disadvantaged children.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants

RE Dismissing the Grissmer study as 'just one experiment' is a rhetorical tactic to avoid adjusting educational ideas in...

The 2024 claim expands the scope of the 2023 assertion to include a lack of studies contradicting the Grissmer study alongside the Kim Transfer Effect.
SK There are zero credible studies that contradict the Kim Transfer Effect regarding the superiority of teaching shared ...
RE There are no credible studies that contradict the findings of the Grissmer study or the Kim Transfer Effect regarding...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Students who possess shared knowledge are able to gain more shared knowledge independently.
The 2024 book repeats the 2023 assertion that shared knowledge facilitates independent learning.
SK Students who possess shared knowledge are able to gain more shared knowledge independently.
RE Students who possess shared knowledge are able to gain more knowledge independently.
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Common topic knowledge within a classroom allows teachers to achieve far transfer for all students efficiently.
The 2024 text adds the descriptor 'efficiently' to the 2023 claim about achieving far transfer through common topic knowledge.
SK Common topic knowledge among students enables teachers to achieve 'far transfer' for all students in a class.
RE Common topic knowledge within a classroom allows teachers to achieve far transfer for all students efficiently.
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
Reading test results cannot be reliably attributed to a language-arts program alone; they are a product of the child's entire curriculum.
The 2024 text reinforces the 2023 point that reading results reflect the entire curriculum rather than just language arts programs.
SK Reading test results cannot be reliably attributed to a language-arts program alone; they are a product of the child'...
RE Reading test results cannot be validly attributed solely to a language arts program without considering the whole cur...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
The key variable in the Grissmer study's success was the shared-topic curriculum, not a specific proprietary language arts program.
The 2024 version clarifies that the shared curriculum's success is independent of 'proprietary language arts programs.'
SK The key variable driving the Grissmer study's positive results was the whole shared-topic curriculum.
RE The key variable in the Grissmer study's success was the shared-topic curriculum, not a specific proprietary language...
77879606101620222324 Repeated 2 variants
To accurately report on reading progress, researchers must account for the child's entire curriculum, not just their socioeconomic status.
The 2024 book repeats the 2023 requirement that researchers must account for the whole curriculum when reporting reading progress.
SK Educational researchers must account for a child's entire curriculum, not just socio-economic status, when reporting ...
RE To accurately report on reading progress, researchers must account for the child's entire curriculum, not just their ...
77879606101620222324 Refined 2 variants
Programs that systematically build knowledge shared by the wider nation yield better reading results than those that do not.
The 2024 version adds that knowledge building must be done 'systematically' to yield better reading results.
SK Elementary programs that build knowledge shared by the wider nation yield better reading results than those that do not.
RE Programs that systematically build knowledge shared by the wider nation yield better reading results than those that ...
77879606101620222324 Broadened 2 variants
The founding ideas of Time magazine were influenced by a confidence in the American future taught in Yale's philosophy and history departments.
The 2024 text generalizes the influence of Yale's departments to 'its graduates' rather than focusing specifically on Time magazine's founders.

77879606101620222324 Evolved 2 variants
In a rational policy environment, achieving better educational results at a lower cost should be sufficient to end the debate and determine school policy.
The 2024 text shifts from a call for 'rational policy' to a critique of 'Romanticism' as the cause of educational confusion.
SK In a rational policy environment, achieving better educational results at a lower cost should be sufficient to end th...
RE Romanticism in educational policy results in confusion, disunion, and illiteracy.
